en: The human nose is an underrated tool. (7 words)
cn: 人类的鼻子是一种被低估的工具。

en: Humans are often thought to be insensitive smellers compared with animals, but this is largely because, unlike animals, we stand upright. (21 words)
cn: 与动物相比，人类常被认为嗅觉迟钝，但这在很大程度上是因为我们直立行走，这一点与动物不同。

en: This means that our noses are confined to perceiving those smells which float through the air, missing the majority of smells which stick to surfaces. (25 words)
cn: 这意味着我们的鼻子只限于感知那些漂浮在空气中的气味，而错过了大部分附着在地表的气味。

en: In fact, though we are extremely sensitive to smells, we do not generally realize it. (15 words)
cn: 事实上，尽管我们对气味极为敏感，但我们通常却意识不到这一点。

en: Our noses are capable of detecting human smells even when these are diluted to far below one part in one million. (21 words)
cn: 我们的鼻子能察觉到稀释到远低于百万分之一的人类气味。

en: Strangely, some people find that they can smell one type of flower but not another, whereas others are sensitive to the smells of both flowers. (25 words)
cn: 奇怪的是，有些人发觉自己能闻到一种花的香味，却闻不到另一种，而另一些人则对这两种花的香味都很敏感。

en: This may be because some people do not have the genes necessary to generate particular smell receptors in the nose. (20 words)
cn: 这可能是因为有些人缺少在鼻腔中生成特定气味感受器所必需的基因。

en: These receptors are the cells which sense smells and send messages to the brain. (14 words)
cn: 这些感受器是那些感知气味并向大脑发送信息的细胞。

en: However, it has been found that even people insensitive to a certain smell at first can suddenly become sensitive to it when exposed to it often enough. (27 words)
cn: 然而，研究发现，即使是最初对某种气味不敏感的人，如果经常接触这种气味，也会突然对其变得敏感起来。

en: The explanation for insensitivity to smell seems to be that the brain finds it inefficient to keep all smell receptors working all the time but can create new receptors if necessary. (31 words)
cn: 对嗅觉不灵敏的解释似乎是，大脑发现让所有气味感受器一直工作效率太低，但可以在必要时创建新的感受器。

en: This may also explain why we are not usually aware of the usual smell of our own house, but we notice new smells when we visit someone else's. (28 words)
cn: 这或许也能解释为什么我们往往察觉不到自己家里的惯常气味，但当我们到别人家拜访时，却会注意到新的气味。

en: The brain finds it best to keep smell receptors available for unfamiliar and emergency signals such as the smell of smoke, which might indicate the danger of fire. (28 words)
cn: 大脑发现，最好让气味感受器随时准备接收不熟悉的信号和紧急信号，比如可能预示着火灾危险的烟雾气味。

en: The homeless make up a growing percentage of America's population. (10 words)
cn: 无家可归者在美国人口中所占的比例日益增大。

en: Indeed, homelessness has reached such proportions that local government can't possibly cope. (12 words)
cn: 事实上，无家可归现象已发展到如此严重的程度，以至于地方政府根本无法应对。

en: To help homeless people toward independence, the federal government must support job training programs, raise the minimum wage, and fund more low-cost housing. (23 words)
cn: 为帮助无家可归者走向自立，联邦政府必须支持就业培训项目、提高最低工资标准，并资助建设更多的廉租房。

en: Not everyone agrees on the number of Americans who are homeless. (11 words)
cn: 对于美国究竟有多少无家可归者，各方意见并不一致。

en: Estimates range anywhere from 600,000 to 3 million. (8 words)
cn: 估计数字从60万到300万不等。

en: Although the figure may vary, analysts do agree on another matter: that the number of the homeless is increasing. (19 words)
cn: 尽管具体数字可能各有不同，但分析人士在另一件事上的确看法一致：无家可归者的人数正在增长。

en: One of the federal government's studies predicts that the number of the homeless will reach nearly 19 million by the end of this decade. (24 words)
cn: 联邦政府的一项研究预测，在这个十年结束之前，无家可归者的人数将达到近1900万。

en: Finding ways to assist this growing homeless population has become increasingly difficult. (12 words)
cn: 寻找途径去帮助这一日益庞大的无家可归群体，已变得愈发困难。

en: Even when homeless individuals manage to find a shelter that will give them three meals a day and a place to sleep at night, a good number still spend the bulk of each day wandering the street. (37 words)
cn: 即使无家可归者设法找到了收容所，能让他们一天吃上三顿饭、晚上有个地方睡觉，相当多的人仍然每天大部分时间都在街上流浪。

en: Part of the problem is that many homeless adults are addicted to alcohol or drugs. (15 words)
cn: 部分问题在于，许多无家可归的成年人沉溺于酒精或毒品。

en: And a significant number of the homeless have serious mental disorders. (11 words)
cn: 而且，相当多的无家可归者患有严重的精神障碍。

en: Many others, not addicted or mentally ill, simply lack the everyday survival skills needed to turn their lives around. (19 words)
cn: 其他许多人，既不酗酒也无精神疾病，却就是缺乏改变生活所需的日常生存技能。

en: Boston Globe reporter Chris Reidy notes that the situation will improve only when there are comprehensive programs that address the many needs of the homeless. (25 words)
cn: 《波士顿环球报》记者克里斯·雷迪指出，只有当实施了能够解决无家可归者诸多需求的综合性项目时，情况才会好转。

en: As Edward Zlotkowski, director of community service at Bentley College in Massachusetts, puts it, "There has to be coordination of programs. (21 words)
cn: 正如马萨诸塞州本特利学院社区服务主任爱德华·兹洛特科夫斯基所言：“各项计划必须协调配合。”

en: What's needed is a package deal. (6 words)
cn: 我们需要的是一揽子方案。

en: By 1830 the former Spanish and Portuguese colonies had become independent nations. (12 words)
cn: 到1830年，原西班牙和葡萄牙的殖民地都已成为了独立国家。

en: The roughly 20 million inhabitants of these nations looked hopefully to the future. (13 words)
cn: 这些国家大约2000万居民满怀希望地展望未来。

en: Born in the crisis of the old regime and Iberian colonialism, many of the leaders of independence shared the ideals of representative government, careers open to talent, freedom of commerce and trade, the right to private property, and a belief in the individual as the basis of society. (48 words)
cn: 许多独立运动领袖诞生于旧政权和伊比利亚殖民主义统治的危机之中，他们都有共同的理想：代议制政府、为有才能之人敞开大门的职业、商贸自由、私有财产权，以及认为个人是社会之基础的信念。

en: Generally, there was a belief that the new nations should be sovereign and independent states, large enough to be economically viable and integrated by a common set of laws. (29 words)
cn: 人们普遍认为，新国家应该是主权独立的国家，大到足以在经济上能够自立，并由一套共同的法律统一起来。

en: On the issue of freedom of religion and the position of the Church, however, there was less agreement among the leadership. (21 words)
cn: 然而，关于宗教自由和教会地位的问题，领导层之间却分歧较大。

en: Roman Catholicism had been the state religion and the only one funded by the Spanish crown. (16 words)
cn: 罗马天主教曾是国教，并且是唯一一个由西班牙王室资助的宗教。

en: While most leaders sought to maintain Catholicism as the official religion of the new states, some sought to end the exclusion of other faiths. (24 words)
cn: 虽然大多数领袖力图维持天主教作为新国家的官方宗教，但也有一些人则力图结束对其他信仰的排斥。

en: The defense of the Church became a rallying cry for the conservative forces. (13 words)
cn: 捍卫教会成了保守势力的战斗口号。

en: The ideals of the early leaders of independence were often egalitarian, valuing equality of everything. (15 words)
cn: 早期独立领袖的理想通常是平等主义，强调一切平等。

en: Bolivar had received aid from Haiti and had promised in return to abolish slavery in the areas he liberated. (19 words)
cn: 玻利瓦尔曾得到海地的援助，并作为回报承诺在他解放的地区废除奴隶制。

en: By 1854, slavery had been abolished everywhere except Spain's remaining colonies. (11 words)
cn: 到1854年，除西班牙残存的殖民地外，各地的奴隶制均已被废除。

en: Early promises to end Indian tribute and taxes on people of mixed origin came much slower because the new nations still needed the revenue such policies produced. (27 words)
cn: 早期关于取消印第安人贡赋和向混血人种征税的承诺，兑现起来要慢得多，因为新国家仍需要这些政策带来的财政收入。

en: Egalitarian sentiments were often tempered by fears that the mass of the population was unprepared for self-rule and democracy. (19 words)
cn: 平等主义的情绪常常由于担心大众尚未准备好实行自治和民主而有所缓和。

en: The idea that some groups of people may be more intelligent than others is one of those hypotheses that dare not speak its name. (24 words)
cn: 认为某些族群可能比其他族群更聪明的观点，是那种不敢直说的假说之一。

en: But Gregory Cochran is prepared to say it anyway. (9 words)
cn: 但格雷戈里·科克伦无论如何都准备这么说。

en: He is that rare bird, a scientist who works independently of any institution. (13 words)
cn: 他是那种稀罕人物，一位不隶属于任何机构的独立科学家。

en: He helped popularize the idea that some diseases not previously thought to have a bacterial cause were actually infections, which aroused much controversy when it was first suggested. (28 words)
cn: 他曾助力推广了这样一种观点：一些此前被认为与细菌无关的疾病，实际上就是感染，这一观点首次提出时引起了极大争议。

en: Even he, however, might tremble at the thought of what he is about to do. (15 words)
cn: 然而，就连他，想到自己即将要做的事，可能也会不寒而栗。

en: Together with another two scientists, he is publishing a paper which not only suggests that one group of humanity is more intelligent than the others, but explains the process that has brought this about. (34 words)
cn: 他正与另两位科学家共同发表一篇论文，该论文不仅提出人类的某一个族群比其他族群更聪明，而且还解释了导致这一结果的过程。

en: The group in question are a particular people originated from central Europe. (12 words)
cn: 论文中提及的群体，是起源于中欧的一个特定民族。

en: The process is natural selection. (5 words)
cn: 这个过程是自然选择。

en: This group generally do well in IQ test, scoring 12-15 points above the mean value of 100, and have contributed disproportionately to the intellectual and cultural life of the West, as the careers of their elites, including several world-renowned scientists, affirm. (41 words)
cn: 这个群体通常在智商测试中表现出色，得分比100分的平均值高出12至15分，并且他们对西方的知识和文化生活做出了远超比例的巨大贡献，这一点从他们中那些精英（包括几位世界知名科学家）所成就的事业便可得证。

en: They also suffer more often than most people from a number of nasty genetic diseases, such as breast cancer. (19 words)
cn: 他们也比大多数人更常罹患一些严重的遗传性疾病，比如乳腺癌。

en: These facts, however, have previously been thought unrelated. (9 words)
cn: 然而，这些情况以前被认为是互不相关的。

en: The former has been put down to social effects, such as a strong tradition of valuing education. (17 words)
cn: 前者被归因于社会影响，比如重视教育的深厚传统。

en: The latter was seen as a consequence of genetic isolation. (11 words)
cn: 而后者则被视为基因隔离的结果。

en: Dr. Cochran suggests that the intelligence and diseases are intimately linked. (12 words)
cn: 科克伦博士指出，智力和疾病是密切相关的。

en: His argument is that the unusual history of these people has subjected them to unique evolutionary pressures that have resulted in this paradoxical state of affairs. (26 words)
cn: 他的论点是，这个民族不寻常的历史让他们承受了独特的进化压力，从而导致了这种看似矛盾的状况。

en: Research on animal intelligence always makes us wonder just how smart humans are. (13 words)
cn: 对动物智力的研究总是让我们思考，人类到底有多聪明。

en: Consider the fruit-fly experiments described by Carl Zimmer in the Science Times. (12 words)
cn: 想想卡尔·齐默在《科学时报》上描述的果蝇实验。

en: Fruit flies who were taught to be smarter than the average fruit fly tended to live shorter lives. (18 words)
cn: 那些被训练得比普通果蝇更聪明的果蝇，其寿命往往更短。

en: This suggests that dimmer bulbs burn longer, that there is an advantage in not being too bright. (17 words)
cn: 这暗示着，光线暗的灯泡燃得更久，不太聪明反倒是一种优势。

en: Intelligence, it turns out, is a high-priced option. (8 words)
cn: 事实证明，智力是一个代价高昂的选择。

en: It takes more upkeep, burns more fuel and is slow off the starting line because it depends on learning—a gradual process—instead of instinct. (25 words)
cn: 它需要更多的维护，消耗更多的能量，而且起步较慢，因为它依赖学习——一个渐进的过程——而非本能。

en: Plenty of other species are able to learn, and one of the things they've apparently learned is when to stop. (20 words)
cn: 很多其他物种也有学习能力，而且它们显然学会了一件事，那就是何时停止。

en: Is there an adaptive value to limited intelligence? (8 words)
cn: 有限的智力是否存在一种适应价值呢？

en: That's the question behind this new research. (7 words)
cn: 这正是此项新研究背后的问题。

en: Instead of casting a wistful glance backward at all the species we've left in the dust I.Q.-wise, it implicitly asks what the real costs of our own intelligence might be. (30 words)
cn: 它没有悲怀地回望那些在智力方面被我们远远甩在身后的所有物种，而是隐晦地追问，我们自身智力的真正代价可能是什么。

en: This is comprehensive the mind of every animal we've ever met. (11 words)
cn: 这是我们所遇到的每一种动物心智的全貌。

en: Research on animal intelligence also makes us wonder what experiments animals would perform on humans if they had the chance. (20 words)
cn: 对动物智力的研究也让我们好奇，如果动物有机会，它们会在人类身上进行什么样的实验。

en: Every cat with an owner, for instance, is running a small-scale study in operant conditioning. (15 words)
cn: 比方说，每只有主人的猫，都在进行着一项关于操作性条件反射的小规模研究。

en: We believe that if intelligent animals ran the labs, they would test us to determine the limits of our patience, our faithfulness, our memory for locations. (26 words)
cn: 我们认为，如果由聪明的动物来管理实验室，它们会测试我们，以确定我们耐心的极限、忠诚度以及对地点的记忆力。

en: They would try to decide what intelligence in humans is really fundamental, not merely how much of it there is. (20 words)
cn: 它们会试图判定，人类的智力中究竟什么才是真正根本的东西，而不仅仅是智力的高低。

en: So far, they would hope to study a fundamental question: Are humans actually aware of the world they live in? (20 words)
cn: 到目前为止，它们希望研究一个根本性的问题：人类是否真正了解自己所生存的这个世界？

en: So far the results are inconclusive. (6 words)
cn: 到目前为止，结果尚无定论。

en: In 1924 America's National Research Council sent two engineers to supervise a series of experiments at a telephone-parts factory called the Hawthorne Plant near Chicago. (25 words)
cn: 1924年，美国国家研究委员会派出两名工程师，前往芝加哥附近一家名为霍桑工厂的电话零件厂，指导一系列实验。

en: It hoped they would learn how shop-floor lighting affected workers' productivity. (11 words)
cn: 委员会希望他们能了解车间照明如何影响工人的生产效率。

en: Instead, the studies ended up giving their name to the "Hawthorne effect", the extremely influential idea that the very sight of being experimented upon changed subjects' behavior. (27 words)
cn: 然而，这些研究最终却为“霍桑效应”这一极具影响力的概念冠了名，该概念认为，仅仅是意识到自己正在被实验这一事实，就足以改变实验对象的行为。

en: The idea arose because of the perplexing behavior of the women in the plant. (14 words)
cn: 这一想法之所以产生，是因为工厂女工们令人费解的行为。

en: According to accounts of the experiments, their hourly output rose when lighting was increased, but also when it was dimmed. (20 words)
cn: 根据实验的描述，照明增强时，她们每小时的产量会提高；但照明变暗时，产量也同样会提高。

en: It did not matter what was done in the experiment; as long as something was changed, productivity rose. (18 words)
cn: 实验中做了什么并不重要；只要有所改变，生产效率就会提高。

en: A perception that they were being experimented upon seemed to be enough to alter workers' behavior on its own. (19 words)
cn: 一种自己正在被实验的感觉，光凭这一点似乎就足以改变工人们的行为。

en: After several decades, the same data were subjected to econometric analysis. (11 words)
cn: 几十年后，同一批数据接受了计量经济学的分析。

en: The Hawthorne experiments had another surprise in store. (8 words)
cn: 霍桑实验还有另一个令人意想不到的结果。

en: Contrary to the descriptions on record, no systematic evidence was found that levels of productivity were related to changes in lighting. (21 words)
cn: 与档案记录中的描述相反，研究人员并没有发现系统性的证据来表明生产效率水平与照明变化相关。

en: It turns out that the peculiar way of conducting the experiments may have led to misleading interpretations of what happened. (20 words)
cn: 事实证明，进行实验的独特方式，可能导致了对所发生事情的误导性解读。

en: For example, lighting was always changed on a Sunday. (9 words)
cn: 例如，照明总是在周日进行调整。

en: When work started again on Monday, output duly rose compared with the previous Saturday and continued to rise for the next couple of days. (24 words)
cn: 当周一重新开工时，与上周六相比，产量适时地上升了，并在接下来的几天里继续攀升。

en: However, a comparison with data for weeks when there was no experimentation showed that output always went up on Mondays. (20 words)
cn: 然而，将之与没有进行实验的几周数据进行比较后发现，产量在周一总是会上升。

en: Workers tended to be diligent for the first few days of the week in any case, before hitting a plateau and then slackening off. (24 words)
cn: 无论如何，工人们往往会在每周的头几天勤奋工作，随后达到瓶颈期，继而松懈下来。

en: This suggests that the alleged "Hawthorne effect" is hard to pin down. (12 words)
cn: 这表明，所谓的“霍桑效应”是难以确证的。

en: Ancient Greek philosopher Aristotle viewed laughter as "a bodily exercise precious to health." (13 words)
cn: 古希腊哲学家亚里士多德将笑视为“一种对身体极为有益的运动”。

en: But despite some claims to the contrary, laughing probably has little influence on physical fitness. (15 words)
cn: 但尽管有些相反的观点，笑对身体健康的影响大概微乎其微。

en: Laughter does produce short-term changes in the function of the heart and its blood vessels, boosting heart rate and oxygen consumption. (20 words)
cn: 笑确实会引起心脏及其血管功能的短期变化，加快心率和氧气消耗。

en: But because hard laughter is difficult to sustain, a good laugh is unlikely to have measurable benefits the way, say, walking or jogging does. (24 words)
cn: 但因为大笑很难持久，一场酣畅淋漓的笑不太可能像散步或慢跑那样，产生可衡量的益处。

en: In fact, instead of straining muscles to build them, as exercise does, laughter apparently accomplishes the opposite. (17 words)
cn: 事实上，运动是通过拉伸肌肉来塑造肌肉，而笑并非如此，它显然达到了相反的效果。

en: Studies dating back to the 1930s indicate that laughter relaxes muscles, decreasing muscle tone for up to 45 minutes after the laugh dies down. (24 words)
cn: 可追溯至20世纪30年代的研究表明，笑能使肌肉放松，在笑声消失后长达45分钟内降低肌肉张力。

en: Such bodily reaction might conceivably help moderate the effects of psychological stress. (12 words)
cn: 可以想象，这种身体反应或许有助于缓解心理压力的影响。

en: Anyway, the act of laughing probably does produce other types of physical feedback that improve an individual's emotional state. (19 words)
cn: 无论如何，笑这一行为很可能确实会产生其他类型的身体反馈，从而改善个体的情绪状态。

en: According to one classical theory of emotion, our feelings are partially rooted in physical reactions. (15 words)
cn: 根据一种经典的情绪理论，我们的情感部分植根于身体反应。

en: It was argued at the end of the 19th century that humans do not cry because they are sad but they become sad when the tears begin to flow. (29 words)
cn: 19世纪末曾有人提出，人不是因为悲伤而哭泣，而是当眼泪开始流淌时才变得悲伤。

en: Although sadness also precedes tears, evidence suggests that emotions can flow from muscular responses. (14 words)
cn: 尽管悲伤也会先于泪水出现，但有证据表明，情绪可以源于肌肉反应。

en: In an experiment published in 1988, social psychologist Fritz Strack of the University of Würzburg in Germany asked volunteers to hold a pen either with their teeth—thereby creating an artificial smile—or with their lips, which would produce a disappointed expression. (42 words)
cn: 在1988年发表的一项实验中，德国维尔茨堡大学的社会心理学家弗里茨·斯特拉克要求志愿者们用牙齿咬住一支笔，从而做出一个假笑的表情；或者用嘴唇含住笔，做出一个失望的表情。

en: Those forced to exercise their smiling muscles reacted more enthusiastically to funny cartoons than did those whose mouths were contracted in a frown, suggesting that expressions may influence emotions rather than just the other way around. (35 words)
cn: 那些被迫调动微笑肌肉的人，对有趣的卡通片反应得比那些嘴巴收缩成皱眉状的人更为热烈，这表明表情或许能影响情绪，而非仅仅是情绪影响表情。

en: Similarly, the physical act of laughter could improve mood. (9 words)
cn: 同理，笑这一身体行为可以改善情绪。

en: The ethical judgments of the Supreme Court justices have become an important issue recently. (14 words)
cn: 最高法院大法官们的道德操守评判，近来已成为一个重要议题。

en: The court cannot maintain its legitimacy as guardian of the rule of law when justices behave like politicians. (18 words)
cn: 当大法官们像政客一样行事时，法院就无法维持其作为法治守护者的正当性。

en: Yet, in several instances, justices acted in ways that weakened the court's reputation for being independent and impartial. (18 words)
cn: 然而，在某些情况下，大法官的行事方式削弱了法院独立且公正的声誉。

en: Justice Antonin Scalia, for example, appeared at political events. (9 words)
cn: 例如，大法官安东宁·斯卡利亚曾在政治活动中现身。

en: That kind of activity makes it less likely that the court's decisions will be accepted as impartial judgments. (18 words)
cn: 此类行为使得法院的裁决不太可能被视作公正的判决而为人所接受。

en: Part of the problem is that the justices are not bound by an ethics code. (15 words)
cn: 问题的部分症结在于，大法官们不受道德准则的约束。

en: At the very least, the court should make itself subject to the code of conduct that applies to the rest of the federal judiciary. (24 words)
cn: 最起码，法院应当让自己遵循那些适用于联邦司法系统其他人员的行为准则。

en: This and other similar cases raise the question of whether there is still a line between the court and politics. (20 words)
cn: 这个以及其他类似案例引发了一个问题：法院与政治之间是否还存在界限。

en: The framers of the Constitution envisioned law as having authority apart from politics. (13 words)
cn: 宪法制定者们设想法律拥有独立于政治的权威。

en: They gave justices permanent positions so they would be free to upset those in power and have no need to cultivate political support. (23 words)
cn: 他们赋予大法官终身职位，这样大法官们就可以毫无顾忌地触怒当权者，也无需去培养政治支持。

en: Our legal system was designed to set law apart from politics precisely because they are so closely tied. (18 words)
cn: 我们的法律制度之所以被设计得让法律与政治分离，恰恰是因为它们二者之间联系太过紧密。

en: Constitutional law is political because it results from choices rooted in fundamental social concepts like liberty and property. (18 words)
cn: 宪法本身就是政治性的，因为它源于那些根植于自由、财产等基本社会观念的选择。

en: When the court deals with social policy decisions, the law it shapes is inescapably political—which is why decisions split along ideological lines are so easily dismissed as unjust. (29 words)
cn: 当法院处理社会政策裁决时，它所塑造的法律就不可避免地带有政治性——这就是为什么那些因意识形态分歧而产生的判决，很容易被指责为不公而遭到摒弃。

en: The justices must address doubts about the court's legitimacy by making themselves accountable to the code of conduct. (18 words)
cn: 大法官们必须通过让自己对行为准则负责，来回应外界对法院正当性的质疑。

en: That would make rulings more likely to be seen as separate from politics and, as a result, convincing as law. (20 words)
cn: 那样一来，裁决就更有可能被视为独立于政治，进而作为法律令人信服。

en: People are, on the whole, poor at considering background information when making individual decisions. (14 words)
cn: 总体而言，人们在做出单个决策时，并不擅长考虑背景信息。

en: At first glance, this might seem like a strength that grants the ability to make judgments which are unbiased by external factors. (22 words)
cn: 乍一看，这似乎像是一种优势，它让人能够做出不受外部因素影响的判断。

en: But Dr. Uri Simonsohn speculated that an inability to consider the big picture was leading decision-makers to be biased by the daily samples of information they were working with. (29 words)
cn: 但乌里·西蒙松博士推测，缺乏对全局的考量，正导致决策者受到他们日常所接触信息样本的偏见影响。

en: For example, he theorized that a judge fearful of appearing too soft on crime might be more likely to send someone to prison if he had already sentenced five or six other defendants only to forced community service on that day. (41 words)
cn: 例如，他提出理论称，一位担心对犯罪行为表现得太过心软的法官，如果在那天已经连续判处了五六个被告仅仅是强制社区服务，那么他就更有可能将下一个人送进监狱。

en: To test this idea, he turned to the university-admissions process. (10 words)
cn: 为了检验这个想法，他将目光转向了大学录取流程。

en: In theory, the success of an applicant should not depend on the few others chosen randomly for an interview during the same day, but Dr. Simonsohn suspected the truth was otherwise. (31 words)
cn: 理论上讲，一个申请者能否成功，不应该取决于同一天中随机被选来面试的其他几名申请者，但西蒙松博士怀疑真相并非如此。

en: He studied the results of 9,323 MBA interviews conducted by 31 admissions officers. (13 words)
cn: 他研究了由31位招生官进行的9323场MBA面试的结果。

en: The interviewers had rated applicants on a scale of one to five. (12 words)
cn: 面试官们按照一到五的等级对申请者进行评分。

en: This scale took numerous factors into consideration. (7 words)
cn: 这个评分等级将众多因素纳入了考量。

en: The scores were then used in conjunction with an applicant's score on the Graduate Management Admission Test, or GMAT, a standardized exam which is marked out of 800 points, to make a decision on whether to accept him or her. (40 words)
cn: 然后，这些分数会与申请者的管理专业研究生入学考试（即GMAT，一种满分为800分的标准化考试）的成绩结合起来，以决定是否录取。

en: Dr. Simonsohn found if the score of the previous candidate in a daily series of interviewees was 0.75 points or more higher than that of the one before that, then the score for the next applicant would drop by an average of 0.075 points. (44 words)
cn: 西蒙松博士发现，在一系列当日的面试者中，如果前一位候选人的分数比他之前那位高出0.75分或更多，那么下一位申请者的分数平均会下降0.075分。

en: This might sound small, but to undo the effects of such a decrease, a candidate would need 30 more GMAT points than would otherwise have been necessary. (27 words)
cn: 这个分值听上去可能很小，但要消除这一降分的影响，申请者的GMAT成绩需要比在其他情况下所需的高出30分。

en: As many people hit middle age, they often start to notice that their memory and mental clarity are not what they used to be. (24 words)
cn: 当许多人步入中年时，他们常常开始注意到自己的记忆力和思维清晰度大不如前。

en: We suddenly can't remember where we put the keys just a moment ago, or an old acquaintance's name, or the name of an old band we used to love. (29 words)
cn: 我们会突然想不起刚刚把钥匙放在了哪里，或者记不起一位老熟人的名字，又或者想不起我们曾经钟爱的一支老乐队之名。

en: As the brain fades, we refer to these occurrences as "senior moments." (12 words)
cn: 随着大脑衰退，我们把这些情况称为“年老忘事”。

en: While seemingly innocent, this loss of mental focus can potentially have a damaging impact on our professional, social, and personal well-being. (21 words)
cn: 这种精神注意力的丧失，看似无害，却可能对我们的职业、社交和个人福祉产生破坏性的影响。

en: Neuroscientists, experts who study the nervous system, are increasingly showing that there's actually a lot that can be done. (19 words)
cn: 研究神经系统的专家——神经科学家们，正越来越多地证明，实际上大有可为。

en: It turns out that the brain needs exercise in much the same way our muscles do, and the right mental workouts can significantly improve our basic cognitive functions. (28 words)
cn: 事实证明，大脑与肌肉一样需要锻炼，而且恰当的脑力训练可以显著改善我们基本的认知功能。

en: Thinking is essentially a process of making connections in the brain. (11 words)
cn: 思考本质上是一个在大脑中建立连接的过程。

en: To a certain extent, our ability to excel in making the connections that drive intelligence is inherited. (17 words)
cn: 在某种程度上，我们擅长建立那些驱动智力的连接的能力，是与生俱来的。

en: However, because these connections are made through effort and practice, scientists believe that intelligence can expand and fluctuate according to mental effort. (22 words)
cn: 然而，由于这些连接是通过努力和练习形成的，科学家们相信，智力可以根据思维活动而扩展和波动。

en: Now, a new Web-based company has taken it a step further and developed the first "brain training program" designed to actually help people improve and regain their mental sharpness. (29 words)
cn: 如今，一家新成立的网络公司更进了一步，开发出了首个“大脑训练计划”，该计划旨在切实帮助人们改善和恢复思维敏锐度。

en: The Web-based program allows you to systematically improve your memory and attention skills. (13 words)
cn: 这个基于网络的程序能让你系统地提高记忆力和注意力。

en: The program keeps track of your progress and provides detailed feedback on your performance and improvement. (16 words)
cn: 该程序会追踪你的进展，并就你的表现和进步提供详细的反馈。

en: Most importantly, it constantly modifies and enhances the games you play to build on the strengths you are developing—much like an effective exercise routine requires you to increase resistance and vary your muscle use. (35 words)
cn: 最重要的是，它会不断调整和强化你所玩的游戏，以巩固你正在发展的优势——这一点很像有效的日常锻炼，需要你不断增加阻力、变换肌肉的运用方式。

en: Though not biologically related, friends are as "related" as fourth cousins, sharing about 1% of genes. (16 words)
cn: 尽管没有生物学上的亲缘关系，但朋友之间就像第四代表亲一样“沾亲带故”，共享约1%的基因。

en: That is what a study, published from the University of California and Yale University in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, has concluded. (25 words)
cn: 这就是一项由加利福尼亚大学和耶鲁大学联合发表在《美国国家科学院院刊》上的研究所得出的结论。

en: The study is a genome-wide analysis conducted on 1,932 unique subjects which compared pairs of unrelated friends and unrelated strangers. (20 words)
cn: 该研究是一项对1932名特殊受试者进行的全基因组分析，它比较了互不相识的朋友对和互不相识的陌生人对。

en: The same people were used in both samples. (8 words)
cn: 两个样本中采用了同一批人。

en: While 1% may seem insignificant, it is not so to a geneticist. (12 words)
cn: 虽然1%看似微不足道，但对遗传学家来说却并非如此。

en: As James Fowler, professor of medical genetics at UC San Diego, says, "Most people do not even know their fourth cousins but somehow manage to select as friends the people who resemble our kin." (34 words)
cn: 正如加州大学圣地亚哥分校的医学遗传学教授詹姆斯·福勒所言：“大多数人甚至不认识自己的第四代表亲，却不知何故总能挑选那些与我们亲属相似的人做朋友。”

en: The study also found that the genes for smell were something shared in friends but not genes for immunity. (19 words)
cn: 该研究还发现，朋友之间共享的是嗅觉基因，而非免疫基因。

en: Why this similarity exists in smell genes is difficult to explain, for now. (13 words)
cn: 为什么在嗅觉基因上会存在这种相似性，目前尚难解释。

en: Perhaps, as the team suggests, it draws us to similar environments but there is more to it. (17 words)
cn: 也许，正如研究小组所表明的那样，它将我们吸引到相似的环境中，但事情并非仅此而已。

en: There could be many mechanisms working together that drive us in choosing genetically similar friends rather than "functional kinship" of being friends with benefits! (24 words)
cn: 可能有许多机制共同作用，驱使我们选择基因相似的朋友，而非那种带有功利目的的“功能性亲密关系”！

en: One of the remarkable findings of the study was that the similar genes seem to be evolving faster than other genes. (21 words)
cn: 该研究的一个引人注目的发现是，这些相似的基因似乎比其他基因进化得更快。

en: Studying this could help understand why human evolution picked pace in the last 30,000 years, with social environment being a major contributory factor. (23 words)
cn: 对此进行研究，或许有助于理解为什么人类的进化在过去3万年中加快了步伐，而社会环境是其中一个重要的促成因素。

en: The findings do not simply explain people's tendency to befriend those of similar ethnic backgrounds, say the researchers. (18 words)
cn: 研究人员说，这些发现并不是简单地解释了人们倾向于与种族背景相似的人交朋友。

en: Though all the subjects were drawn from a population of European extraction, care was taken to see that all subjects, friends and strangers, were taken from the same population. (29 words)
cn: 尽管所有受试者都来自欧洲血统的人群，但研究人员还是格外小心，以确保所有受试者，无论是朋友还是陌生人，均来自同一人群。

en: In Cambodia, the choice of a spouse is a complex one for the young male. (15 words)
cn: 在柬埔寨，择偶对年轻男性来说是一件复杂的事情。

en: It may involve not only his parents and his friends, as well as those of the young woman, but also a matchmaker. (22 words)
cn: 这不仅可能涉及男女双方各自的父母和朋友，还可能涉及媒人。

en: A young man can decide on a likely spouse on his own and then ask his parents to arrange the marriage negotiations, or the young man's parents may make the choice of a spouse, giving the child little to say in the selection. (43 words)
cn: 年轻男子可以自行决定合适的配偶，然后请父母安排提亲；或者，年轻男子的父母也可能直接选定儿媳人选，而在这选择过程中，孩子几乎没有发言权。

en: In theory, a girl may veto the spouse her parents have chosen. (12 words)
cn: 理论上，女孩可以否决父母为其选定的配偶。

en: After a spouse has been selected, each family investigates the other to make sure its child is marrying into a good family. (22 words)
cn: 择定对象之后，双方家庭会互相调查，以确保自家孩子是与一个体面人家结亲。

en: The traditional wedding is a long and colorful affair. (9 words)
cn: 传统婚礼是一个漫长且丰富多彩的仪式。

en: Formerly it lasted three days, but by the 1980s it more commonly lasted a day and a half. (18 words)
cn: 以前婚礼要持续三天，但到了20世纪80年代，通常只持续一天半。

en: Buddhist priests offer a short sermon and recite prayers of blessing. (11 words)
cn: 佛教僧侣会作简短的布道，并诵念祈福的经文。

en: Parts of the ceremony involve ritual hair cutting, tying cotton threads soaked in holy water around the bride's and groom's wrists, and passing a candle around a circle of happily married and respected couples to bless the union. (38 words)
cn: 仪式的部分环节包括：象征性的剪发；将浸过圣水的棉线系在新娘和新郎的手腕上；以及，在一圈婚姻美满、受人尊敬的夫妇间传递蜡烛，以祝福这对新人的结合。

en: Newlyweds traditionally move in with the wife's parents and may live with them up to a year, until they can build a new house nearby. (25 words)
cn: 按照传统，新婚夫妇会搬去与妻子的父母同住，并可能与他们一起居住长达一年，直到他们能在附近建起一座新房为止。

en: Divorce is legal and easy to obtain, but not common. (10 words)
cn: 离婚是合法的，也容易获批，但并不常见。

en: Divorced persons are viewed with some disapproval. (7 words)
cn: 离异者会被人投以几分非议。

en: Each spouse retains whatever property he or she brought into the marriage, and jointly-acquired property is divided equally. (18 words)
cn: 夫妻双方各自保留婚前带来的财产，婚后共同购置的财产则平均分配。

en: Divorced persons may remarry, but a gender prejudice shows up: The divorced male doesn't have a waiting period before he can remarry while the woman must wait ten months. (29 words)
cn: 离婚者可以再婚，但一种性别偏见显现出来：离婚男子无需等待期便可再婚，而女子则必须等满十个月。

en: Could a hug a day keep the doctor away? (9 words)
cn: 一天一个拥抱，能让医生远离我吗？

en: The answer may be a resounding "yes!" (7 words)
cn: 答案或许是一个响亮的“是的！”

en: Besides helping you feel close and connected to people you care about, it turns out that hugs can bring a host of health benefits to your body and mind. (29 words)
cn: 除了能帮你感觉与在乎的人更亲近、更紧密之外，事实证明，拥抱还能给你的身心健康带来诸多益处。

en: Believe it or not, a warm embrace might even help you avoid getting sick this winter. (16 words)
cn: 信不信由你，一个温暖的拥抱甚至可能帮助你在这个冬天免于生病。

en: In a recent study involving over 400 healthy adults, researchers from Carnegie Mellon University in Pennsylvania examined the effects of perceived social support and the receipt of hugs on the participants' susceptibility to developing the common cold after being exposed to the virus. (43 words)
cn: 在近期一项涉及400多名健康成年人的研究中，宾夕法尼亚州卡内基梅隆大学的研究人员们考察了感受到的社会支持和接受拥抱，对参与者在接触感冒病毒后患上普通感冒的易感性的影响。

en: People who perceived greater social support were less likely to come down with a cold, and the researchers calculated that the stress-reducing effects of hugging explained about 32 percent of that beneficial effect. (33 words)
cn: 那些感受到更多社会支持的人，患上感冒的可能性更低，研究人员通过计算得出，拥抱减轻压力的效果，大约解释了32%的这种有益效应。

en: Even among those who got a cold, the ones who felt greater social support and received more frequent hugs had less severe symptoms. (23 words)
cn: 即使在那些患上感冒的人当中，那些感受到更多社会支持和得到更频繁拥抱的人，其症状也相对较轻。

en: "Hugging protects people who are under stress from the increased risk for colds that's usually associated with stress," notes Sheldon Cohen, a professor of psychology at Carnegie. (27 words)
cn: 卡内基梅隆大学的心理学教授谢尔登·科恩指出：“拥抱能保护那些处于压力之下的人，使其免于患上通常与压力相关的感冒的更高风险。”

en: Hugging "is a marker of intimacy and helps generate the feeling that others are there to help in the face of difficulty." (22 words)
cn: 拥抱“是亲密感的一种标志，并且有助于催生这样一种感觉：在困难面前，有其他人会伸出援手。”

en: Some experts attribute the stress-reducing, health-related benefits of hugging to the release of oxytocin, often called "the bonding hormone" because it promotes attachment in relationships, including that between mothers and their newborn babies. (33 words)
cn: 一些专家将拥抱所具有的减压及健康功效，归因于催产素的释放；催产素常被称为“联结激素”，因为它能促进关系中的依恋，包括母亲和新生儿之间的情感联结。

en: Oxytocin is made primarily in the central lower part of the brain, and some of it is released into the bloodstream. (21 words)
cn: 催产素主要在大脑中下部生成，其中一些会被释放到血液中。

en: But some of it remains in the brain, where it influences mood, behavior, and physiology. (15 words)
cn: 但有些催产素仍留存在大脑中，在那里影响着情绪、行为和生理机能。

en: Trust is a tricky business. (5 words)
cn: 信任是一件微妙的事情。

en: On the one hand, it's a necessary condition for many worthwhile things: child care, friendships, etc. (16 words)
cn: 一方面，它是许多宝贵之事的必要条件：比如育儿、友谊等等。

en: On the other hand, putting your faith in the wrong place often carries a high price. (16 words)
cn: 另一方面，信错对象往往要付出高昂的代价。

en: Then, why do we trust at all? (7 words)
cn: 那么，我们究竟为何要去信任呢？

en: Well, because it feels good. (5 words)
cn: 嗯，因为感觉很好。

en: When people place their trust in an individual or an institution, their brains release oxytocin, a hormone that produces pleasurable feelings and triggers the herding instinct that prompts humans to connect with one another. (34 words)
cn: 当人们信任某个人或某个机构时，大脑会释放催产素，这是一种能产生愉悦感并触发群居本能的激素，这种本能促使人们彼此建立联系。

en: Scientists have found that exposure to this hormone puts us in a trusting mood: In a Swiss study, researchers sprayed oxytocin into the noses of half the subjects; those subjects were ready to lend significantly higher amounts of money to strangers than were their counterparts who inhaled something else. (49 words)
cn: 科学家发现，接触这种激素会让我们处于信任的情绪中：在瑞士的一项研究中，研究人员将催产素喷入半数的受试者鼻中；这些受试者准备借给陌生人的钱款数额，明显高于吸入其他东西的对照组。

en: Lucky for us, we also have a sixth sense for dishonesty that may protect us. (15 words)
cn: 幸运的是，我们对不诚实行为还有第六感，这或许能保护我们。

en: A Canadian study found that children as young as 14 months can differentiate between a credible person and a dishonest one. (21 words)
cn: 加拿大的一项研究发现，小至14个月大的孩子就能区分值得信赖的人和不诚实的人。

en: Sixty toddlers were each introduced to an adult tester holding a plastic container. (13 words)
cn: 60名学步幼童被逐一介绍给一位手持塑料容器的成年测试员。

en: The tester would ask, "What's in here?" before looking into the container, smiling, and exclaiming, "Wow!" Each subject was then invited to look inside. (24 words)
cn: 测试员会问：“这里面是什么呀？”然后朝容器里看看，笑着说，“哇！”接着就邀请每个孩子往里看。

en: Half of them found a toy; the other half discovered the container was empty—and realized the tester had fooled them. (21 words)
cn: 半数孩子发现了一个玩具；另外半数孩子则发现容器是空的——并意识到测试员骗了他们。

en: Among the children who had not been tricked, the majority were willing to cooperate with the tester in learning a new skill, demonstrating that they trusted his leadership. (28 words)
cn: 在那些没有被戏弄的孩子中，大多数人愿意配合测试员学习一项新技能，这表明他们信任他的主导。

en: In contrast, only five of the 30 children paired with the "unreliable" tester participated in a follow-up activity. (18 words)
cn: 相比之下，与“不可靠”测试员配对的30名孩子中，只有5人参加了一项后续活动。

en: Today, we live in a world where GPS systems, digital maps, and other navigation apps are available on our smart phones. (21 words)
cn: 如今，我们生活在这样一个世界里：GPS系统、数字地图以及各种导航应用，在智能手机上触手可及。

en: Few of us just walk straight into the woods without a phone. (12 words)
cn: 我们中几乎没有人会不带手机就一头扎进森林。

en: But phones run on batteries, and batteries can die faster than we realize. (13 words)
cn: 但手机要靠电池驱动，而电池的消耗速度往往比我们预想的快得多。

en: If you get lost without a phone or a compass, and you literally can't find north, a few tricks may help you navigate back to civilization, one of which is to follow the land. (34 words)
cn: 如果你在既没有手机也没有指南针的情况下迷了路，真的辨不清方向，有几个小技巧或许能帮你找到回归文明的路，其中之一便是顺着地势走。

en: When you find yourself off a trail, but not in a completely unfamiliar area of land, you have to answer two questions: Which way is downhill, in this particular area? (30 words)
cn: 当你发现自己偏离了小路，但所处之地又并非全然陌生时，你需要回答两个问题：在这片区域，下坡的方向在哪里？

en: And where is the nearest water source? (7 words)
cn: 最近的水源又在何处？

en: Humans overwhelmingly live in valleys, and on supplies of fresh water. (11 words)
cn: 人类绝大多数聚居于山谷之中，依赖淡水为生。

en: So, if you head downhill, and follow any H2O you find, you should eventually see signs of people. (18 words)
cn: 因此，如果你一路向下，沿途追寻任何水源，终究应该会看到人迹。

en: If you've explored the area before, keep an eye out for familiar sights—you may be surprised how quickly identifying a distinctive rock or tree can restore your bearings. (29 words)
cn: 如果你曾经在这一带探索过，那就留心寻找熟悉的景物——你也许会惊讶地发现，认出一块特别的岩石或一棵与众不同的树，竟能如此迅速地让你重新找到方向。

en: Another option: Climb high and look for signs of human habitation. (11 words)
cn: 还有另一个办法：登高望远，寻找人类居住的迹象。

en: For example, even in dense forest, you should be able to spot gaps in the tree line due to roads, train tracks, and other paths people carve through the woods. (30 words)
cn: 比如，即便身处茂密的林中，你也应该能发现林木线上的缺口——那是人们开辟道路、铺设铁轨以及开凿其他通道所留下的痕迹。

en: Head toward these breaks to find a way out. (9 words)
cn: 朝着这些缺口走去，便能找到出路。

en: At night, scan the horizon for artificial light sources, such as fires and streetlights, then walk toward the glow of light pollution. (22 words)
cn: 夜间，仔细搜寻地平线上的人造光源，比如篝火或路灯的光亮，然后循着那片光污染的辉光走去。

en: Finally, assuming you're lost in an area humans tend to frequent, look for the marks we leave on the landscape. (20 words)
cn: 最后，假设你迷失在一片人类时常涉足的区域，那就寻找我们在大地上留下的印记。

en: Trail blazes, tire tracks, and other features can lead you to civilization. (12 words)
cn: 树皮上的刻痕、轮胎压出的车辙，以及其他种种痕迹，都能引领你回到文明之地。

en: Even if families don't sit down to eat together as frequently as before, millions of Britons will nonetheless have got a share this weekend of one of the nation's great traditions: the Sunday roast. (34 words)
cn: 即使如今家人围坐在一起吃饭不像以前那么频繁了，但在这个周末，数百万英国人仍会分享到这个国家伟大传统之一：周日烤肉。

en: On a cold winter's day, few culinary pleasures can match it. (11 words)
cn: 在寒冷的冬日，很少有烹饪之乐能与之相比。

en: Yet as we report now, the food police are determined that this enjoyment should be rendered yet another guilty pleasure guaranteed to damage our health. (25 words)
cn: 然而，正如我们此刻所报道的，“食品警察”执意要将这种享受变成另一种必定损害健康的罪恶快感。

en: The Food Standards Authority (FSA) has issued a public warning about the risks of a compound called acrylamide that forms in some foods cooked at high temperatures. (27 words)
cn: 食品标准局已就一种名为丙烯酰胺的化合物的风险发布公开警告，这种化合物是在某些食物经高温烹制时形成的。

en: This means that people should avoid crisping their roast potatoes, reject thin-crust pizzas and only partially toast their bread. (19 words)
cn: 这意味着人们应该避免把烤土豆做得过于酥脆，拒绝薄底比萨，并且只把面包烤到微黄。

en: But where is the evidence to support such alarmist advice? (10 words)
cn: 但是，支持这种危言耸听建议的证据何在？

en: While studies have shown that acrylamide can cause neurological damage in mice, there is no conclusive evidence that it causes cancer in humans. (23 words)
cn: 虽然研究表明，丙烯酰胺会对小鼠造成神经损伤，但并无确凿证据表明它会致人类患癌。

en: Scientists say the compound is likely to cause cancer but have no hard scientific proof. (15 words)
cn: 科学家称这种化合物可能会致癌，但尚无确凿的科学证据。

en: On the basis of the precautionary principle, it could be argued that it is advisable to follow the FSA advice. (20 words)
cn: 基于预防性原则，可以说遵照食品标准局的建议是明智之举。

en: After all, it was rumored that smoking caused cancer for years before the evidence was found to prove a connection. (20 words)
cn: 毕竟，早在证据被发现、证实吸烟与癌症有关联之前，就有谣言称吸烟致癌多年了。

en: Doubtless a piece of boiled beef can always be served up on Sunday alongside some steamed vegetables, without the Yorkshire pudding and no wine. (24 words)
cn: 毫无疑问，周日总可以端上一块白煮牛肉，配上些蒸蔬菜，没有约克郡布丁，也没有葡萄酒。

en: But would life be worth living? (6 words)
cn: 但这样过日子还值得吗？

en: To be fair, the FSA says it is not telling people to cut out roast foods entirely, but to reduce their lifetime intake. (23 words)
cn: 说句公道话，食品标准局表示，它并非让人们完全禁绝烤制食物，而是减少终身摄入量。

en: However, its campaign risks coming across as being pushy and overprotective. (11 words)
cn: 然而，它的宣传活动恐怕会给人留下专制和过度保护的印象。

en: Constant health scares just end up with no one listening. (10 words)
cn: 持续不断的健康恐慌，最终只会换来无人理睬。

en: Fluid intelligence is the type of intelligence that has to do with short-term memory and the ability to think quickly, logically, and abstractly in order to solve new problems. (29 words)
cn: 流体智力是一种与短期记忆相关，以及快速、有逻辑且抽象地思考以解决新问题的能力有关的智力类型。

en: It peaks in young adulthood, levels out for a period of time, and then generally starts to slowly decline as we age. (22 words)
cn: 它在青年时期达到顶峰，经过一段平台期后，通常随着我们年龄增长而开始缓慢下降。

en: But while aging is inevitable, scientists are finding out that certain changes in brain function may not be. (18 words)
cn: 但是，虽说衰老无可避免，科学家们却发现，大脑功能的某些改变却未必如此。

en: One study found that muscle loss and the accumulation of body fat around the abdomen are associated with a decline in fluid intelligence. (23 words)
cn: 一项研究发现，肌肉的流失和腹部脂肪的堆积与流体智力的下降有关。

en: This suggests the possibility that lifestyle factors might help prevent or delay this type of decline. (16 words)
cn: 这暗示了一种可能性，即生活方式的因素也许有助于预防或延缓这种类型的衰退。

en: The researchers looked at data that included measurements of lean muscle and abdominal fat from more than 4,000 middle-to-older-aged men and women and compared that data to reported changes in fluid intelligence over a six-year period. (36 words)
cn: 研究人员查阅了涵盖4000多名中老年男女的瘦肌肉量和腹部脂肪测量值的数据，并将这些数据与六年期间里报告的流体智力变化进行了比较。

en: They found that middle-aged people with higher measures of abdominal fat scored worse on measures of fluid intelligence as the years went by. (23 words)
cn: 他们发现，随着岁月推移，腹部脂肪测量值较高的中年人，在流体智力测试中的得分更差。

en: For women, the association may be attributable to changes in immunity that resulted from excess abdominal fat; in men, the immune system did not appear to be involved. (28 words)
cn: 对女性而言，这一关联可能归因于腹部脂肪过多引发的免疫系统变化；而在男性中，免疫系统似乎并未参与其中。

en: It is hoped that future studies could explain these differences and perhaps lead to different treatments for men and women. (20 words)
cn: 希望未来的研究能够解释这些差异，或许还能为男性和女性带来不同的治疗方法。

en: Meanwhile, there are steps you can take to help reduce abdominal fat and maintain lean muscle mass as you age in order to protect both your physical and mental well-being. (30 words)
cn: 与此同时，您可以采取一些措施，在年龄增长的同时，帮助减少腹部脂肪、保持瘦肌肉量，以保护身心健康。

en: The two highly recommended lifestyle approaches are maintaining or increasing your level of aerobic exercise and following a Mediterranean-style diet that is high in fiber and eliminates highly processed foods. (30 words)
cn: 两个备受推荐的生活方式是：维持或提高你的有氧运动水平；以及，遵循富含纤维、杜绝高度加工食品的地中海式饮食。

en: The idea that plants have some degree of consciousness first took root in the early 2000s; the term "plant neurobiology" was coined around the notion that some aspects of plant behavior could be compared to intelligence in animals. (37 words)
cn: 植物具有某种程度上的意识这一想法，最早扎根于21世纪初；“植物神经生物学”这个术语，是围绕植物行为的某些方面或可与动物的智力进行比较这一观念而创立的。

en: Though plants lack brains, the firing of electrical signals in their stems and leaves nonetheless triggered responses that hinted at consciousness, researchers previously reported. (24 words)
cn: 研究人员此前报告称，虽然植物没有大脑，但其茎叶中电信号的激增，却触发了暗示意识存在的反应。

en: But such an idea is untrue, according to a new opinion article. (12 words)
cn: 但根据一篇新的观点文章，这种想法是不正确的。

en: Plant biology is complex and fascinating, but it differs so greatly from that of animals that so-called evidence of plants' intelligence is inconclusive, the authors wrote. (27 words)
cn: 文章作者写道，植物生物学复杂且引人入胜，但它与动物生物学的差异如此巨大，以至于所谓的植物智力证据是缺乏定论的。

en: Beginning in 2006, some scientists have argued that plants possess neuron-like cells that interact with hormones and neurotransmitters, forming "a plant nervous system, analogous to that in animals," said lead study author Lincoln Taiz, "They even claimed that plants have "brain-like command centers" at their root tips. (47 words)
cn: 该研究的第一作者林肯·塔伊兹称，从2006年开始，一些科学家就主张，植物拥有与激素和神经递质相互作用的、类似神经元的细胞，形成了“一个类似于动物神经系统的植物神经系统”，“他们甚至声称植物根尖有‘类似大脑的指挥中心’。”

en: This perspective makes sense if you simplify the workings of a complex brain, reducing it to an array of electrical pulses; cells in plants also communicate through electrical signals. (29 words)
cn: 如果你将一个复杂大脑的运作方式简单化，将其归结为一系列电脉冲，那么这种观点就说得通；植物中的细胞也通过电信号进行交流。

en: However, the signaling in a plant is only superficially similar to the billions in a complex animal brain, which is more than "a mass of cells that communicate by electricity," Taiz said. (31 words)
cn: 然而，塔伊兹说，植物中的信号传导，与复杂动物大脑中数以十亿计的信号，只是表面上相似，动物大脑远不止是“一团通过电流进行交流的细胞”。

en: For consciousness to evolve, a brain with a threshold level of complexity and capacity is required, he added. (18 words)
cn: 他补充说，要进化出意识，需要有达到一定复杂度与容量门槛的大脑。

en: Since plants don't have nervous systems, the chances that they have consciousness are effectively zero. (15 words)
cn: 既然植物没有神经系统，那么它们拥有意识的可能性实际上为零。

en: And what's so great about consciousness, anyway? (7 words)
cn: 再说了，意识又有什么了不起的呢？

en: Plants can't run away from danger, so investing energy in a body system which recognizes a threat and can feel pain would be a very poor evolutionary strategy, according to the article. (32 words)
cn: 根据该文章，植物无法逃离危险，因此将能量投入到一个能识别威胁并感受到疼痛的身体系统中，将是一种非常糟糕的进化策略。

en: Caravanserais were roadside inns that were built along the Silk Road in areas including China, North Africa, and the Middle East. (21 words)
cn: 商队驿站是沿丝绸之路而建的沿途客栈，分布在中国、北非和中东等地区。

en: They were typically located outside the walls of a city or village and were usually funded by local governments or privately. (21 words)
cn: 它们通常坐落在城镇或村庄的城墙之外，一般由地方政府出资或由私人出资修建。

en: The word "caravanserai" is a transition of the Persian words "kārvān," which means a group of travelers or a caravan, and "sarāy," a palace or enclosed building. (27 words)
cn: “Caravanserai”一词由波斯语“kārvān”（意为一群旅行者或商队）和“sarāy”（意为宫殿或封闭建筑）组合演变而来。

en: The term caravan was used to describe groups of people who traveled together across the ancient network for safety reasons, such as merchants, travelers, or pilgrims. (26 words)
cn: 商队一词，用来指为了安全起见，结伴穿越古代网络旅行的群体，如商人、旅人或香客。

en: From the 10th century onwards, as merchant and travel routes became more developed, the construction of caravanserais increased, and they served as a safe place for people to rest at night. (31 words)
cn: 从10世纪起，随着商路和旅行线路日趋发达，商队驿站的建设也日渐增多，成为人们夜间休憩的安全之所。

en: Travelers on the Silk Road faced the possibility of being attacked by thieves or being subjected to extreme weather conditions. (20 words)
cn: 丝绸之路上的旅者，面临着遭遇盗贼袭击或经受极端天气状况的可能。

en: For this reason, caravanserais were strategically placed so that they could be reached in a day's travel time. (18 words)
cn: 出于这个原因，商队驿站被战略性地设置在一日行程可以抵达的范围内。

en: Caravanserais served as an informal meeting point for the various people who traveled the Silk Road. (16 words)
cn: 商队驿站是行走在丝绸之路上的各色人等非正式的集会点。

en: As a result, these structures became important centers for cultural exchange and interaction, with travelers sharing their cultures, ideas, and beliefs, as well as taking knowledge with them, greatly influencing the development of several civilizations. (35 words)
cn: 结果，这些建筑成了文化交流与互动的重要中心，旅行者们在此分享各自的文化、观念和信仰，也将知识随身带走，对诸多文明的发展产生了深远影响。

en: Caravanserais were also an important marketplace for commodities and aided in the trade of goods along the Silk Road. (19 words)
cn: 商队驿站也是重要的商品市场，促进了丝绸之路沿线的货物贸易。

en: Rather, it was frequently the first stop for merchants looking to sell their wares and stock up on supplies for their own journeys. (23 words)
cn: 更确切地说，它往往是商人们沿途想要兜售货物、并为自己的旅程补给物资的首站。

en: It is believed that around 12,000 to 15,000 caravanserais were built along the Silk Road, although only about 3,000 are known to remain today, many of which are in ruins. (30 words)
cn: 据信，沿丝绸之路修建的商队驿站约有1.2万至1.5万座，不过，如今已知仅存的大约只有3000座，其中许多已沦为废墟。

en: There's nothing more welcoming than a door opening for you. (10 words)
cn: 没有什么比一扇门为你开启更让人感到欢迎的了。

en: Without the need to be touched to open or close, automatic doors are essential in improving disabled access to buildings and helping provide general convenience to commercial buildings. (28 words)
cn: 自动门无需触碰便能开关，在改善残障人士进出建筑物的便利，以及为商业建筑提供普遍便利方面，至关重要。

en: Self-sliding doors began to emerge as a commercial product in 1960 after being invented six years previously by Americans Dee Horton and Lew Hewitt. (24 words)
cn: 自动滑动门由美国人迪伊·霍顿和卢·休伊特在六年前发明，之后于1960年开始作为商业产品面世。

en: They started out as a novelty feature, but as their use has grown, their benefits have extended within our technologically advanced world. (22 words)
cn: 它们最初是作为一种新颖的特色出道的，但随着其使用日益广泛，在我们这个技术先进的世界里，它们的益处也在不断扩展。

en: Particularly useful in busy locations or during times of emergency, the doors act as crowd management by reducing the obstacles put in people's way. (24 words)
cn: 在繁忙的场所或紧急时刻，这些门格外有用，它们通过减少人们行进中的障碍，起到了疏导人群的作用。

en: As well as making access both in and out of buildings easier for people, the difference in the way many of these doors open helps reduce the total area occupied by them. (32 words)
cn: 除了让人们进出建筑更加容易之外，许多此类门开启方式的不同，也有助于减少它们所占据的总面积。

en: Automatic doors often open to the side, with the panels sliding across one another. (14 words)
cn: 自动门通常向侧面打开，门扇互相交错滑动。

en: Replacing swing doors, these allow smaller buildings to maximize the usable space inside without having to clear the way for a large, sticking-out door. (24 words)
cn: 取代了平开门之后，这些门让较小的建筑能够最大限度地利用内部可用空间，而不必为一扇外突的大开门腾出空间。

en: There are many different types of automatic door, with each relying on specific signals to tell them when to open. (20 words)
cn: 自动门有许多不同的类型，每一种都依靠特定的信号来告知何时开启。

en: Although these methods differ, the main principles remain the same. (10 words)
cn: 尽管这些方法各不相同，但其主要原理是相通的。

en: Each automatic door system analyses the light, sound, weight or movement in their vicinity as a signal to open. (19 words)
cn: 每个自动门系统都分析其附近区域的光线、声音、重量或运动，以此作为开启的信号。

en: Sensor types are chosen to complement the different environments they are needed in. (13 words)
cn: 传感器的选型，是为了适应其所需服役的不同环境。

en: For example, a busy street might not suit a motion-sensored door, as it would constantly be opening for passers-by. (19 words)
cn: 例如，繁忙的街道可能不适合运用动作感应门，因为它会不断地为路人开启。

en: A pressure sensitive mat would be more appropriate to limit the surveyed area. (13 words)
cn: 压力感应垫则更为合适，用以限定感应区域。

en: Located in the southern Peloponnesian peninsula, Pavlopetri (the modern name of the site) emerged as a Neolithic settlement around 3500 B.C. and became an important trading center for Mycenaean Greece (1650–1180 B.C.).
cn: 帕夫洛佩特里（该遗址的现代名称）位于伯罗奔尼撒半岛南部，约在公元前3500年作为新石器时代聚落兴起，并成为迈锡尼希腊（公元前1650年–公元前1180年）的一个重要贸易中心。

en: This area of the Aegean Sea is prone to earthquakes and tsunamis, which caused the city to gradually sink.
cn: 爱琴海的这片海域易发生地震和海啸，导致这座城市逐渐下沉。

en: The slow sea level rise in the Mediterranean submerged the city more than 3,000 years ago.
cn: 3000多年前，地中海缓慢的海平面上升淹没了这座城市。

en: For millennia, the city's remains lay unseen below some 13 feet of water.
cn: 数千年来，这座城市的遗迹一直隐藏在大约13英尺深的水下，不为人知。

en: They were covered by a thick layer of sand off the island of Laconia.
cn: 它们被拉科尼亚岛近海处一层厚厚的沙子所覆盖。

en: In recent decades, shifting currents and climate change have eroded a natural barrier that protected Pavlopetri.
cn: 近几十年来，不断变化的洋流和气候变化侵蚀了曾保护帕夫洛佩特里的一道天然屏障。

en: In 1967 a scientific survey of the Peloponnesian coast was gathering data to analyze changes in sea levels when British oceanographer Nicholas Flemming first spotted the sunken structures.
cn: 1967年，一项对伯罗奔尼撒海岸的科学考察正在收集数据以分析海平面变化，当时英国海洋学家尼古拉斯·弗莱明首次发现了这些沉没的建筑。

en: A year later, he returned with a few students to examine the location and map the site.
cn: 一年后，他带着几名学生重返此地，对遗址位置进行勘测并绘制了地图。

en: The team identified some 15 buildings, courtyards, a network of streets, and two chamber tombs.
cn: 该团队辨识出了约15座建筑、若干庭院、一套街道网络以及两座墓室。

en: Despite the exciting initial finds, the site would lie undisturbed for decades before archaeologists would return.
cn: 尽管这些初步发现令人振奋，但在考古学家重返此地之前，该遗址将会沉寂数十年而不被扰动。

en: In 2009 archaeologists Chrysanthi Gallou and Jon Henderson resumed the excavation of Pavlopetri in cooperation with the Greek Ministry of Culture.
cn: 2009年，考古学家克里桑蒂·加卢和乔恩·亨德森与希腊文化部合作，重启了帕夫洛佩特里的发掘工作。

en: Since the 1960s, underwater archaeology techniques and tools had made huge advances.
cn: 自20世纪60年代以来，水下考古的技术和工具取得了巨大进步。

en: The team employed robotics, sonar mapping, and state-of-the-art graphics to survey the site.
cn: 团队运用了机器人技术、声纳测绘和最先进的影像来勘测该遗址。

en: From 2009 to 2013 they were able to bring the underwater town to light.
cn: 从2009年到2013年，他们终于让这座水下沉没的城市重见天日。

en: Covering about two and a half acres, Pavlopetri's three main roads connected some 50 rectangular buildings, all of which had open courtyards.
cn: 帕夫洛佩特里占地约两英亩半，其三条主要街道连通了约50座矩形建筑，这些建筑均设有露天庭院。

en: Excavations revealed a large number of Minoan-style loom weights, suggesting Pavlopetri was a thriving trade center with a robust textile industry.
cn: 发掘工作揭示出大量米诺斯风格的织机重锤，表明帕夫洛佩特里曾是一个繁荣的贸易中心，拥有兴旺的纺织业。

en: Advances in artificial intelligence (AI) are rapidly changing every aspect of human life.
cn: 人工智能（AI）的进步正迅速改变着人类生活的方方面面。

en: The world of AI is buzzing with an exciting potential to improve and enrich our lives.
cn: AI领域充斥着改善和丰富我们生活的激动人心的潜力。

en: Still, AI also has the potential hazard of dominating our experiences in ways we might find difficult to control.
cn: 尽管如此，AI也存在着一种潜在危险，即以我们可能难以控制的方式主宰我们的体验。

en: One such area is how we understand and experience beauty.
cn: 其中一个这样的领域就是我们如何理解和体验美。

en: AI can be a collaborative tool in a wide range of creative endeavors.
cn: AI可以在广泛的创造性活动中充当协作工具。

en: Combining human creativity and AI algorithms can lead to unique artistic outcomes that are beautiful to the human eye.
cn: 将人类创造力与AI算法相结合，可以产生人眼看来很美的独特艺术成果。

en: These collaborations are likely to become increasingly common.
cn: 这类协作很可能会变得越来越普遍。

en: For instance, as convenient and provocative, AI enables virtual try-on experiences where you can virtually test makeup, hairstyles, clothing, and even cosmetic procedures before making any physical changes.
cn: 例如，既便捷又诱人，AI实现了虚拟试穿体验，在做出任何实际改变之前，你可以虚拟地测试妆容、发型、服装，甚至整容手术。

en: Individuals can now experiment with different looks and explore their preferences, potentially expanding the range of beauty ideals.
cn: 如今，人们可以尝试不同的造型，探索自己的偏好，从而潜在地拓宽美的理想范围。

en: AI algorithms can analyze facial features and skin conditions to provide personalized beauty recommendations.
cn: AI算法可以分析面部特征和皮肤状况，以提供个性化的美容建议。

en: This customized approach aims to cater to individual preferences and enhance the concept of beauty tailored to each person's unique characteristics.
cn: 这种定制化的方法旨在迎合个人偏好，并强化为每个人独特个性量身定制的美之理念。

en: In this way, AI can be a fun vehicle for self-discovery.
cn: 这样一来，AI可以成为自我发现的有趣载体。

en: While AI offers exciting possibilities, it also raises ethical concerns.
cn: 虽然AI提供了令人兴奋的可能性，但它也引发了伦理上的担忧。

en: There is a risk of deepening societal beauty pressures and perpetuating unattainable beauty standards.
cn: 存在一种风险，即加深社会上的美丽压力，并使那些难以企及的美丽标准长期延续。

en: Additionally, AI-powered beauty filters and editing tools can lead to distorted self-perception and contribute to body dissatisfaction.
cn: 此外，AI驱动的美颜滤镜和修图工具可能导致扭曲的自我认知，并加剧对身体的不满。

en: As summarized in a recent post on "The Hidden Dangers of Online Beauty Filters", relying on this technology for social presentation can cause harm such as body image issues, lower self-esteem, and social anxiety.
cn: 正如最近一篇关于《网络美颜滤镜的隐藏危险》的帖子所总结的，依赖这种技术进行社交展示可能造成伤害，例如身体意象问题、自尊心降低和社交焦虑。

en: It's important to note that while AI can enhance our appreciation of beauty, it should not replace the genuine human experience and the emotional connections we derive from seeing the beauty in each other.
cn: 需要着重指出的是，尽管AI能增进我们对美的欣赏，但它不应取代真实的人类体验，以及我们从看到彼此身上之美而获得的情感联结。

en: The outbreak of swine flu that was first detected in Mexico was declared a global epidemic on June 11, 2009. (20 words)
cn: 最先在墨西哥被发现的猪流感爆发，于2009年6月11日被宣布为全球流行病。

en: It is the first worldwide epidemic designated by the World Health Organization in 41 years. (15 words)
cn: 这是世界卫生组织41年来认定的首例全球性流行病。

en: The heightened alert followed an emergency meeting with flu experts in Geneva that assembled after a sharp rise in cases in Australia, and rising sums in Britain, Japan, Chile and elsewhere. (31 words)
cn: 随着澳大利亚病例数的急剧攀升，以及英国、日本、智利和其他地区病例数的不断增长，流感专家在日内瓦召开了紧急会议，随后警戒级别便提高了。

en: But the epidemic is "moderate" in severity, according to Margaret Chan, the organization's director general, with the overwhelming majority of patients experiencing only mild symptoms and a full recovery, often in the absence of any medical treatment. (37 words)
cn: 但据世卫组织总干事陈冯富珍称，这场流行病的严重程度“温和”，绝大多数患者仅有轻微症状并完全康复，常常无需任何治疗。

en: The outbreak came to global notice in late April 2009, when Mexican authorities noted an unusually large number of hospitalizations and deaths among healthy adults. (25 words)
cn: 此次爆发于2009年4月下旬引起全球关注，当时墨西哥当局注意到，健康成年人中的住院和死亡人数多得不同寻常。

en: As much of Mexico City shut down at the height of a panic, cases began to crop up in New York City, the southwestern United States and around the world. (30 words)
cn: 在恐慌最为严重的时刻，墨西哥城大部分地区都停摆了，而病例却开始在纽约市、美国西南部乃至世界各地纷纷出现。

en: In the United States, new cases seemed to fade as warmer weather arrived. (13 words)
cn: 在美国，随着天气转暖，新增病例似乎减少了。

en: But in late September 2009, officials reported there was significant flu activity in almost every state and that virtually all the samples tested are the new swine flu, also known as (A) H1N1, not seasonal flu. (36 words)
cn: 但在2009年9月下旬，官方报告称几乎每个州都出现了显著的流感活动，且几乎所有检测样本都是新型猪流感——也称为甲型H1N1流感——而非季节性流感。

en: In the U.S., it has infected more than one million people, and caused more than 600 deaths and more than 6,000 hospitalizations. (22 words)
cn: 在美国，它已感染了100多万人，造成600多人死亡，6000多人住院。

en: Federal health officials released Tamiflu for children from the national stockpile and began taking orders from the states for the new swine flu vaccine. (24 words)
cn: 联邦卫生官员从国家储备中发放了用于儿童的达菲，并开始接收各州对新型猪流感疫苗的订单。

en: The new vaccine, which is different from the annual flu vaccine, is available ahead of expectations. (16 words)
cn: 这种不同于年度流感疫苗的新疫苗，比预期提前上市了。

en: More than three million doses were to be made available in early October 2009, though most of those initial doses were of the FluMist nasal spray type, which is not recommended for pregnant women, people over 50 or those with breathing difficulties, heart disease or several other problems. (49 words)
cn: 2009年10月初，将有300多万剂疫苗可用，尽管这些首批疫苗大多是FluMist鼻喷剂型，不建议孕妇、50岁以上人士，或有呼吸困难、心脏病及其他几种问题的人群使用。

en: But it was still possible to vaccinate people in other high-risk groups: health care workers, people caring for infants and healthy young people. (23 words)
cn: 但仍有可能为其他高风险群体接种：医护人员、照料婴儿者以及健康的年轻人。

en: The Internet affords anonymity to its users, a blessing to privacy and freedom of speech. (15 words)
cn: 互联网为用户提供了匿名性，这对隐私和言论自由来说是一件好事。

en: But that very anonymity is also behind the explosion of cyber-crime that has swept across the Web. (17 words)
cn: 但正是这种匿名性，也助推了席卷网络的犯罪活动的激增。

en: Can privacy be preserved while bringing safety and security to a world that seems increasingly lawless? (16 words)
cn: 在让一个看似日益无法无天的世界变得安全的同时，隐私还能得到保护吗？

en: Last month, Howard Schmidt, the nation's cyber-czar, offered the federal government a proposal to make the Web a safer place—a "voluntary trusted identity" system that would be the high-tech equivalent of a physical key, a fingerprint and a photo ID card, all rolled into one. (46 words)
cn: 上个月，美国网络总管霍华德·施密特向联邦政府提交了一份旨在让网络更安全的提案——一个“自愿可信身份”系统，该系统将是实体钥匙、指纹和带照片身份证三者的高科技结合体。

en: The system might use a smart identity card, or a digital credential linked to a specific computer, and would authenticate users at a range of online services. (27 words)
cn: 该系统或许会采用智能身份证，或与特定计算机关联的数字证书，并在一系列在线服务中对用户进行身份验证。

en: The idea is to create a federation of private online identity systems. (12 words)
cn: 其构想是建立一个由私营在线身份系统组成的联盟。

en: Users could select which system to join, and only registered users whose identities have been authenticated could navigate those systems. (20 words)
cn: 用户可以选择加入哪个系统，并且只有身份经过验证的注册用户才能通行于那些系统。

en: The approach contrasts with one that would require an Internet driver's license issued by the government. (16 words)
cn: 这一做法与一种要求政府颁发互联网驾照的方式形成了对比。

en: Google and Microsoft are among companies that already have these "single sign-on" systems that make it possible for users to log in just once but use many different services. (29 words)
cn: 谷歌和微软等公司已经拥有了这类“单次登录”系统，让用户只需登录一次就能使用许多不同的服务。

en: In effect, the approach would create a "walled garden" in cyberspace, with safe "neighborhoods" and bright "streetlights" to establish a sense of a trusted community. (25 words)
cn: 实际上，这一做法会在网络空间里建起一个“围墙花园”，用安全的“社区”和明亮的“路灯”来营造一种可信社区的感觉。

en: Mr. Schmidt described it as a "voluntary ecosystem" in which "individuals and organizations can complete online transactions with confidence, trusting the identities of each other and the identities of the infrastructure on which the transaction runs." (36 words)
cn: 施密特先生将它描述为一个“自愿生态系统”，在其中，“个人和组织可以放心地完成在线交易，信赖彼此的身份以及交易运行所依托的基础设施的身份。”

en: Still, the administration's plan has divided privacy rights activists. (9 words)
cn: 尽管如此，政府的这项计划却在隐私权活动人士中产生了分歧。

en: Some applaud the approach; others are concerned. (7 words)
cn: 有些人为此鼓掌叫好，另一些人则忧心忡忡。

en: It seems clear that such a scheme is an initiative push toward what would eventually be a compulsory Internet "driver's license" mentality. (22 words)
cn: 似乎很明显，这样一种方案是朝着最终强制推行互联网“驾照”思维迈出的一步。

en: The plan has also been greeted with skepticism by some computer security experts, who worry that the "voluntary ecosystem" envisioned by Mr. Schmidt would still leave much of the Internet vulnerable. (31 words)
cn: 该计划也遭到了一些计算机安全专家的质疑，他们担心施密特先生构想的“自愿生态系统”仍会让互联网的大部分地区不堪一击。

en: They argue that all Internet users should be forced to register and identify themselves, in the same way that drivers must be licensed to drive on public roads. (28 words)
cn: 他们主张，所有互联网用户都应被强制注册并表明身份，正如司机必须在公共道路上行驶须持有驾照一样。

en: Millions of Americans and foreigners see G. I. Joe as a mindless war toy, the symbol of American military adventurism, but that's not how it used to be. (28 words)
cn: 数以百万计的美国人和外国人将G.I. Joe视为一种没有头脑的战争玩具，美国军事冒险主义的象征，但过去并非如此。

en: To the men and women who served in World War Ⅱ and the people they liberated, the G.I. was the common man grown into hero, the poor farm kid torn away from his home, the guy who bore all the burdens of battle, who slept in cold foxholes, who went without the necessities of food and shelter, who stuck it out and drove back the Nazi reign of murder. (69 words)
cn: 对于那些在二战中服役的男女士兵以及他们所解放的人民来说，G.I.是成长为英雄的普通人，是那个被迫离家的贫苦农家子弟，是那个承受着战争全部重担的人，他睡在冰冷的散兵坑里，没有食物和住所等必需品，却坚持到底，击退了纳粹的屠杀统治。

en: This was not a volunteer soldier, not someone well paid, but an average guy, up against the best trained, best equipped, fiercest, most brutal enemies seen in centuries. (28 words)
cn: 这不是一名志愿兵，也不是什么报酬优厚的人，而是一个普通的小伙子，面对的是几个世纪以来训练最有素、装备最精良、最凶猛、最残忍的敌人。

en: His name isn't much. (4 words)
cn: 他的名字没什么了不起。

en: G.I. is just a military abbreviation meaning Government Issue, and it was on all of the articles handed out to soldiers. (21 words)
cn: G.I.只是一个军事缩写，意为“政府配发”，它印在所有分发给士兵的物品上。

en: And Joe? (2 words)
cn: 那Joe呢？

en: A common name for a guy who never made it to the top. (13 words)
cn: 一个从未出人头地的人的普通名字。

en: Joe Blow, Joe Palooka, Joe Magrac...a working class name. (9 words)
cn: Joe Blow, Joe Palooka, Joe Magrac……一个工人阶级的名字。

en: The United States has never had a president or vice-president or secretary of state named Joe. (16 words)
cn: 美国从未有过叫Joe的总统、副总统或国务卿。

en: G.I. Joe had a distinguished career fighting German, Japanese, and Korean troops. (12 words)
cn: G.I. Joe在与德国、日本和韩国军队的战斗中有着卓越的生涯。

en: He appears as a character, or a collection of American personalities, in the 1945 movie The Story of G.I. Joe, based on the last days of war correspondent Ernie Pyle. (19 words)
cn: 在1945年的电影《G.I. Joe的故事》中，他作为一个角色，或者说一群美国人个性的集合体出现，这部电影改编自战地记者厄尼·派尔最后的岁月。

en: Some of the soldiers Pyle interviewed portrayed themselves in the film. (11 words)
cn: 派尔采访过的一些士兵在影片中本色出演。

en: Pyle was famous for covering the human side of the war, writing about the dirt-snow-and-mud soldiers, not how many miles were gained or what towns were captured or liberated. (29 words)
cn: 派尔以报道战争中的人性面而闻名，他写的是那些满身泥土、冰雪和淤泥的士兵，而不是推进了多少英里，或攻占解放了哪些城镇。

en: His reports paralleled the "Willie" cartoons of famed Stars and Stripes artist Bill Maulden. (14 words)
cn: 他的报道与著名的《星条旗报》画家比尔·毛尔登的“威利”系列漫画有异曲同工之妙。

en: Both men emphasized the dirt and exhaustion of war, the fragments of civilization that the soldiers shared with each other and the civilians: coffee, tobacco, whiskey, shelter, sleep. (28 words)
cn: 两人都着意刻画战争的泥泞与疲惫，以及士兵们彼此之间、与平民之间共享的文明碎片：咖啡、烟草、威士忌、栖身处、睡眠。

en: Among Egypt, France, and a dozen more countries, G.I. Joe was any American soldier, at that point the most important person in their lives. (24 words)
cn: 在埃及、法国以及其他十几个国家，G.I. Joe就是任何一个美国士兵，在那时是他们生命中最重要的人。

en: Given the advantages of electronic money, you might think that we would move quickly to the cashless society in which all payments are made electronically. (25 words)
cn: 鉴于电子货币的优势，你或许会认为我们将迅速迈向一个所有支付都以电子方式完成的无现金社会。

en: However, a true cashless society is probably not around the corner. (11 words)
cn: 然而，一个真正的无现金社会大概不会很快到来。

en: Indeed, predictions have been around for two decades but have not yet come to fruition. (15 words)
cn: 事实上，这种预测已存在了二十年，却至今仍未实现。

en: For example, Business Week predicted in 1975 that electronic means of payment would soon "revolutionize the very concept of money itself," only to reverse itself several years later. (28 words)
cn: 例如，《商业周刊》曾在1975年预言，电子支付手段将很快“彻底改变货币本身的概念”，结果仅仅几年后就自己推翻了这一说法。

en: Why has the movement to a cashless society been so slow in coming? (13 words)
cn: 为什么迈向无现金社会的进程如此缓慢？

en: Although electronic means of payment may be more efficient than a payments system based on paper, several factors work against the disappearance of the paper system. (26 words)
cn: 虽然电子支付手段可能比基于纸质的支付系统更高效，但有几个因素阻碍着纸质系统的消亡。

en: First, it is very expensive to set up the computer, card reader, and telecommunications networks necessary to make electronic money the dominant form of payment. (25 words)
cn: 首先，建立让电子货币成为主导支付形式所必需的计算机、读卡器和电信网络，耗资非常巨大。

en: Second, paper checks have the advantage that they provide receipts, something that many consumers are unwilling to give up. (19 words)
cn: 第二，纸质支票的一大优势在于它能提供收据，而这是许多消费者不愿放弃的东西。

en: Third, the use of paper checks gives consumers several days of "float"—it takes several days before a check is cashed and funds are withdrawn from the issuer's account, which means that the writer of the check can earn interest on the funds in the meantime. (46 words)
cn: 第三，使用纸质支票能给消费者带来几天的“在途资金”——支票兑现、资金从出票人账户中被划走需要几天时间，这意味着出票人可以在此期间赚取这笔资金的利息。

en: Because electronic payments are immediate, they eliminate the float for the consumer. (12 words)
cn: 由于电子支付是即时的，它消除了消费者的在途资金。

en: Fourth, electronic means of payment may raise security and privacy concerns. (11 words)
cn: 第四，电子支付手段可能引发安全和隐私方面的担忧。

en: We often hear media reports that an unauthorized hacker has been able to access a computer database and to alter information stored there. (23 words)
cn: 我们经常听到媒体报道说，未经授权的黑客得以侵入计算机数据库，并篡改了存储在那里的信息。

en: The fact that this is not an uncommon occurrence means that dishonest persons might be able to access bank accounts in electronic payments systems and steal from someone else's accounts. (30 words)
cn: 这类事件并不罕见这一事实意味着，不法分子或许能够侵入电子支付系统中的银行账户，并窃取他人账户中的资金。

en: The prevention of this type of fraud is no easy task, and a new field of computer science is developing to cope with security issues. (25 words)
cn: 防范此类欺诈并非易事，计算机科学中的一个新领域正在发展，以应对这些安全问题。

en: A further concern is that the use of electronic means of payment leaves an electronic trail that contains a large amount of personal data. (24 words)
cn: 另一个担忧是，使用电子支付手段会留下包含大量个人数据的电子痕迹。

en: There are concerns that government, employers, and marketers might be able to access these data, thereby violating our privacy. (19 words)
cn: 有人担心，政府、雇主和营销人员或许能够获取这些数据，从而侵犯我们的隐私。

en: Thinner isn't always better. (4 words)
cn: 瘦并不总是更好。

en: A number of studies have concluded that normal-weight people are in fact at higher risk of some diseases compared to those who are overweight. (24 words)
cn: 大量研究已经得出结论：与超重者相比，体重正常的人实际上患上某些疾病的风险更高。

en: And there are health conditions for which being overweight is actually protective. (12 words)
cn: 而且在某些健康状况中，超重实际上具有保护作用。

en: For example, heavier women are less likely to develop calcium deficiency than thin women. (14 words)
cn: 例如，较重的女性比较瘦的女性更不容易出现缺钙状况。

en: Likewise, among the elderly, being somewhat overweight is often an indicator of good health. (14 words)
cn: 同样，在老年人中，稍微偏胖往往是身体健康的一个标志。

en: Of even greater concern is the fact that obesity turns out to be very difficult to define. (17 words)
cn: 更令人担忧的是，事实证明肥胖非常难以定义。

en: It is often defined in terms of body mass index, or BMI. (12 words)
cn: 它通常以身体质量指数，即BMI，来界定。

en: BMI equals body mass divided by the square of height. (10 words)
cn: BMI等于体重除以身高的平方。

en: An adult with a BMI of 18 to 25 is often considered to be normal weight. (16 words)
cn: BMI在18到25之间的成年人通常被认为是标准体重。

en: Between 25 and 30 is overweight. (6 words)
cn: 25到30之间为超重。

en: And over 30 is considered obese. (6 words)
cn: 超过30则被视为肥胖。

en: Obesity, in turn, can be divided into moderately obese, severely obese, and very severely obese. (15 words)
cn: 而肥胖又可依次分为中度肥胖、重度肥胖和极重度肥胖。

en: While such numerical standards seem straightforward, they are not. (9 words)
cn: 尽管这些数字标准看似简单明了，实则不然。

en: Obesity is probably less a matter of weight than body fat. (11 words)
cn: 肥胖大概与其说是体重问题，不如说是体脂问题。

en: Some people with a high BMI are in fact extremely fit, while others with a low BMI may be in poor shape. (22 words)
cn: 有些BMI很高的人实际上非常健壮，而另一些BMI较低的人则可能身体状况很差。

en: For example, many collegiate and professional football players qualify as obese, though their percentage body fat is low. (18 words)
cn: 例如，许多大学和职业橄榄球运动员都达到了肥胖的标准，尽管他们的体脂率很低。

en: Conversely, someone with a small frame may have high body fat but a normal BMI. (15 words)
cn: 相反，骨架小的人可能体脂很高，BMI却正常。

en: Today we have a tendency to label obesity as a disgrace. (11 words)
cn: 如今，我们倾向于将肥胖视为一种耻辱。

en: The overweight are sometimes pictured in the media with their faces covered. (12 words)
cn: 超重者有时被媒体拍到时会遮住面孔。

en: Stereotypes associated with obesity include laziness, lack of willpower, and lower prospects for success. (14 words)
cn: 与肥胖相关的刻板印象包括懒惰、缺乏意志力以及成功前景黯淡。

en: Teachers, employers, and health professionals have been shown to harbor biases against the obese. (14 words)
cn: 教师、雇主和健康专业人士已被证实对肥胖者抱有偏见。

en: Even very young children tend to look down on the overweight, and teasing about body build has long been a problem in schools. (23 words)
cn: 就连很小的孩子也往往看不起超重者，而针对体型的嘲弄长期以来一直是学校里的一个问题。

en: Negative attitudes toward obesity, grounded in health concerns, have stimulated a number of anti-obesity policies. (15 words)
cn: 基于健康担忧而对肥胖持负面态度，这催生了多项反肥胖政策。

en: My own hospital system has banned sugary drinks from its facilities. (11 words)
cn: 我所在的医疗系统已在其设施中禁售含糖饮料。

en: Many employers have instituted weight loss and fitness initiatives. (9 words)
cn: 许多雇主也发起了减肥和健身计划。

en: Michelle Obama launched a high-visibility campaign against childhood obesity, even claiming that it represents our greatest national security threat. (19 words)
cn: 米歇尔·奥巴马发起了一场高调的反儿童肥胖运动，甚至声称它构成了我们最严重的国家安全威胁。

en: In our contemporary culture, the prospect of communicating with—or even looking at—a stranger is virtually unbearable. (18 words)
cn: 在我们当代的文化中，与陌生人交流——甚至只是看他们一眼——的前景，几乎是令人无法忍受的。

en: Everyone around us seems to agree by the way they cling to their phones, even without a signal on a subway. (21 words)
cn: 我们身边的每个人似乎都认同这一点，从他们紧抓着手机不放的样子就能看出，哪怕是在没有信号的地铁里。

en: It's a sad reality—our desire to avoid interacting with other human beings—because there's much to be gained from talking to the stranger standing by you. (27 words)
cn: 这是一个可悲的现实——我们渴望避免与他人互动——因为和站在你身边的陌生人交谈，本可以收获良多。

en: But you wouldn't know it, plugged into your phone. (9 words)
cn: 但你对此一无所知，因为你沉浸在自己的手机里。

en: This universal protection sends the message: "Please don't approach me." (10 words)
cn: 这种普遍的保护姿态传递出这样的信息：“请别靠近我。”

en: What is it that makes us feel we need to hide behind our screens? (14 words)
cn: 是什么让我们觉得需要躲在自己的屏幕后面？

en: One answer is fear, according to Jon Wortmann, executive mental coach. (11 words)
cn: 据高级心理教练乔恩·沃特曼所言，一个答案是恐惧。

en: We fear rejection, or that our innocent social advances will be misinterpreted as "weird." (14 words)
cn: 我们害怕被拒绝，或者害怕自己单纯善意的社交示好会被误解为“古怪”。

en: We fear we'll be judged. (5 words)
cn: 我们害怕被人评判。

en: We fear we'll be disruptive. (5 words)
cn: 我们害怕自己会打扰到别人。

en: Strangers are inherently unfamiliar to us, so we are more likely to feel anxious when communicating with them compared with our friends and acquaintances. (24 words)
cn: 陌生人对我们来说本质上是生疏的，因此与朋友和熟人相比，和他们交流时我们更容易感到焦虑。

en: To avoid this uneasiness, we turn to our phones. (9 words)
cn: 为了避免这种不安，我们转向了手机。

en: "Phones become our security blanket," Wortmann says. (7 words)
cn: “手机成了我们的安抚物，”沃特曼说。

en: "They are our happy glasses that protect us from what we perceive is going to be more dangerous." (18 words)
cn: “它们是我们快乐的屏障，保护我们免受我们自认为会更危险的事情的伤害。”

en: But once we rip off the band-aid, tuck our smartphones in our pockets and look up, it doesn't hurt so bad. (21 words)
cn: 但一旦我们撕掉这层保护罩，把智能手机塞进口袋，抬起头来，其实也并没有那么痛苦。

en: In one 2011 experiment, behavioral scientists Nicholas Epley and Juliana Schroeder asked commuters to do the unthinkable: Start a conversation. (20 words)
cn: 在2011年的一项实验中，行为科学家尼古拉斯·埃普利和朱莉安娜·施罗德让通勤者做了一件无法想象的事：开启一段对话。

en: They had Chicago train commuters talk to their fellow passengers. (10 words)
cn: 他们让芝加哥列车上的通勤者与同车乘客交谈。

en: "When Dr. Epley and Ms. Schroeder asked other people in the same train station to predict how they would feel after talking to a stranger, the commuters thought their ride would be more pleasant if they sat on their own." (40 words)
cn: “当埃普利博士和施罗德女士让同一火车站里的其他人预测，与陌生人交谈后自己会有什么感觉时，这些通勤者认为，如果自己独坐，旅途会更愉快。”

en: The New York Times summarizes. (5 words)
cn: 《纽约时报》总结道。

en: Though the participants didn't expect a positive experience, after they went through with the experiment, "not a single person reported having been embarrassed." (23 words)
cn: 尽管参与者并未预期会有积极的体验，但在他们完成实验后，“没有一个人反馈说自己感到尴尬。”

en: In fact, these commutes were reportedly more enjoyable compared with those without communication, which makes absolute sense, since human beings thrive off of social connections. (25 words)
cn: 事实上，据报道，这些通勤旅途相比那些没有交流的旅途更令人愉快，而这完全说得通，因为人类正是靠社交联结才得以蓬勃发展的。

en: It's that simple: Talking to strangers can make you feel connected. (11 words)
cn: 就这么简单：与陌生人交谈能让你感受到被联结。

en: Happy people work differently. (4 words)
cn: 快乐的人工作方式不同。

en: They're more productive, more creative, and willing to take greater risks. (11 words)
cn: 他们更高效，更有创造力，而且愿意承担更大的风险。

en: And new research suggests that happiness might influence how firms work, too. (12 words)
cn: 而且新的研究表明，快乐或许也会影响公司的运作方式。

en: Companies located in places with happier people invest more, according to a recent research paper. (15 words)
cn: 根据最近的一篇研究论文，位于居民更具幸福感地区的公司投资更多。

en: In particular, firms in happy places spend more on R&D (research and development). (13 words)
cn: 特别是，幸福之地的公司在研发上投入更多。

en: That's because happiness is linked to the kind of longer-term thinking necessary for making investments for the future. (18 words)
cn: 那是因为，快乐与那种为未来进行投资所需的长期思维相关联。

en: The researchers wanted to know if the optimism and inclination for risk-taking that come with happiness would change the way companies invested. (22 words)
cn: 研究人员想弄清楚，伴随快乐而来的乐观和冒险倾向，是否会改变公司的投资方式。

en: So they compared U.S. cities' average happiness measured by Gallup polling with the investment activity of publicly traded firms in those areas. (22 words)
cn: 因此，他们将盖洛普民调测得的美国城市平均幸福度，与那些地区上市公司的投资活动进行了比较。

en: Sure enough, firms' investment and R&D intensity were correlated with the happiness of the area in which they were headquartered. (20 words)
cn: 果不其然，公司的投资和研发强度与其总部所在地的幸福度是相关的。

en: But is it really happiness that's linked to investment, or could something else about happier cities explain why firms there spend more on R&D? (24 words)
cn: 但是，与投资相关联的真的是幸福吗，还是说，更幸福城市的其他某些因素可以解释那里的公司为何在研发上投入更多？

en: To find out, the researchers controlled for various factors that might make firms more likely to invest—like size, industry, and sales—and for indicators that a place was desirable to live in, like growth in wages or population. (39 words)
cn: 为了找到答案，研究人员控制了可能促使公司增加投资的多种因素——比如规模、行业和销售额——以及一个地方宜居程度的指标，如工资或人口的增长。

en: The link between happiness and investment generally held even after accounting for these things. (14 words)
cn: 即便将这些因素都考虑在内，幸福与投资之间的关联大体上依然成立。

en: The correlation between happiness and investment was particularly strong for younger firms, which the authors attribute to "less codified decision-making process" and the possible presence of "younger and less experienced managers who are more likely to be influenced by sentiment." (40 words)
cn: 幸福与投资之间的相关性在较年轻的公司中尤为明显，作者将此归因于“不那么成文的决策过程”，以及可能存在“更年轻、经验较少、更容易受情绪影响的经理人”。

en: The relationship was also stronger in places where happiness was spread more equally. (13 words)
cn: 这种关系在幸福分布更均匀的地方也更为显著。

en: Firms seem to invest more in places where most people are relatively happy, rather than in places with happiness inequality. (20 words)
cn: 公司似乎在大多数人相对幸福的地方投资更多，而非在存在幸福感不平等的地方。

en: While this doesn't prove that happiness causes firms to invest more or to take a longer-term view, the authors believe it at least hints at that possibility. (27 words)
cn: 虽然这并不能证明幸福会导致公司增加投资或采取更长远的眼光，但作者认为，它至少暗示了这种可能性。

en: It's not hard to imagine that local culture and sentiment would help shape how executives think about the future. (19 words)
cn: 不难想象，当地的文化和情感氛围会影响高管们看待未来的方式。

en: "It surely seems plausible that happy people would be more forward-thinking and creative and lean towards R&D more than the average," said one researcher. (24 words)
cn: 一位研究人员说：“快乐的人会比普通人更具前瞻性和创造力，并且更倾向于研发，这确实看似合理。”

en: People have speculated for centuries about a future without work. (10 words)
cn: 几个世纪以来，人们一直在猜测一个没有工作的未来。

en: Today is no different, with academics, writers, and activists once again warning that technology is replacing human workers. (18 words)
cn: 今天也不例外，学者、作家和活动家们再一次发出警告：技术正在取代人类劳动者。

en: Some imagine that the coming work-free world will be defined by inequality: A few wealthy people will own all the capital, and the masses will struggle in an impoverished wasteland. (30 words)
cn: 有人猜想，即将到来的无工作世界将以不平等为特征：少数富人将拥有全部资本，而大众将在贫困的荒原上挣扎。

en: A different and not mutually exclusive prediction holds that the future will be a wasteland of a different sort, one characterized by purposelessness: Without jobs to give their lives meaning, people will simply become lazy and depressed. (37 words)
cn: 另一种并不相互排斥的预测则认为，未来将是另一种荒原，其特征是漫无目的：没有了赋予生活意义的工作，人们只会变得懒惰和抑郁。

en: Indeed, today's unemployed don't seem to be having a great time. (11 words)
cn: 确实，今天的失业者日子似乎并不好过。

en: One Gallup poll found that 20 percent of Americans who have been unemployed for at least a year report having depression, double the rate for working Americans. (27 words)
cn: 一项盖洛普民调发现，失业至少一年的美国人中有20%称自己患有抑郁症，这是在业美国人比例的两倍。

en: Also, some research suggests that the explanation for rising rates of mortality, mental-health problems, and addiction among poorly-educated, middle-aged people is a shortage of well-paid jobs. (26 words)
cn: 此外，一些研究表明，受教育程度低的中年人群中死亡率、心理健康问题和成瘾问题的上升，其解释之一是缺乏高薪工作。

en: Perhaps this is why many worry about the agonizing dullness of a jobless future. (14 words)
cn: 也许这就是为什么许多人担心无工作未来那种令人痛苦的沉闷乏味。

en: But it doesn't necessarily follow from findings like these that a world without work would be filled with unease. (19 words)
cn: 但从这些发现中并不必然得出，一个没有工作的世界将充满不安。

en: Such visions are based on the downsides of being unemployed in a society built on the concept of employment. (19 words)
cn: 此类设想建立在以就业观念为基础的社会中失业的负面效应之上。

en: In the absence of work, a society designed with other ends in mind could yield strikingly different circumstances for the future of labor and leisure. (25 words)
cn: 在没有工作的情况下，一个在设计时心中就另有目标的社会，可能会为未来的劳动和休闲营造出截然不同的境况。

en: Today, the virtue of work may be a bit overblown. (10 words)
cn: 如今，工作的美德或许被有些夸大了。

en: "Many jobs are boring, degrading, unhealthy, and a waste of human potential," says John Danaher, a lecturer at the National University of Ireland in Galway. (25 words)
cn: “许多工作无聊、有辱人格、不健康，而且是对人类潜能的一种浪费，”爱尔兰国立高威大学讲师约翰·达纳赫说。

en: These days, because leisure time is relatively scarce for most workers, people use their free time to counterbalance the intellectual and emotional demands of their jobs. (26 words)
cn: 如今，由于对多数工作者而言闲暇时间相对稀缺，人们便利用空闲时间来抵消工作中的智力和情感消耗。
