en: "When I come home from a hard day's work, I often feel tired," Danaher says, adding, "In a world in which I don't have to work, I might feel rather different"—perhaps different enough to throw himself into a hobby or a passion project with the intensity usually reserved for professional matters. (52 words)
cn: “辛苦工作一天回到家时，我常常感到疲倦，”达纳赫补充说，“在一个我不必工作的世界里，我可能会感觉相当不同”——或许不同到足以让他以通常留给职业事务的强度，投身于某项爱好或一个热衷的项目。

en: Why do people read negative Internet comments and do other things that will obviously be painful? (16 words)
cn: 为什么人们会去读负面的互联网评论，以及做其他明显会令人痛苦的事情？

en: Because humans have an inherent need to resolve uncertainty, according to a recent study in Psychological Science. (17 words)
cn: 根据《心理科学》杂志最近的一项研究，是因为人类有着消除不确定性的内在需求。

en: The new research reveals that the need to know is so strong that people will seek to satisfy their curiosity even when it is clear the answer will hurt. (29 words)
cn: 这项新研究揭示出，这种求知的需求如此强烈，以至于即使答案显然会令人受伤，人们也会设法满足自己的好奇心。

en: In a series of four experiments, behavioral scientists at the University of Chicago and the Wisconsin School of Business tested students' willingness to expose themselves to unpleasant stimuli in an effort to satisfy curiosity. (34 words)
cn: 在一系列的四项实验中，芝加哥大学和威斯康星商学院的行为科学家测试了学生为满足好奇心而甘愿将自己暴露于不愉快刺激下的意愿。

en: For one trial, each participant was shown a pile of pens that the researcher claimed were from a previous experiment. (20 words)
cn: 在一项试验中，每位参与者面前都摆了一堆笔，研究人员称这些笔是之前一项实验留下的。

en: The twist? (2 words)
cn: 出人意料之处何在？

en: Half of the pens would deliver an electric shock when clicked. (11 words)
cn: 其中一半的笔在按下时会产生电击。

en: Twenty-seven students were told which pens were electrified; another twenty-seven were told only that some were electrified. (17 words)
cn: 27名学生被告知哪些笔是带电的；另外27名学生仅被告知其中有些笔带电。

en: When left alone in the room, the students who did not know which ones would shock them clicked more pens and incurred more shocks than the students who knew what would happen. (32 words)
cn: 当被独自留在房间里时，那些不知道哪支笔会电击他们的学生，比那些知道会发生什么的学生，按下了更多的笔，也因此遭受了更多的电击。

en: Subsequent experiments reproduced this effect with other stimuli, such as the sound of fingernails on a chalkboard and photographs of disgusting insects. (22 words)
cn: 随后的实验用其他刺激物重现了这一效应，例如指甲划过黑板的声音和令人作呕的昆虫照片。

en: The drive to discover is deeply rooted in humans, much the same as the basic drives for food or shelter, says Christopher Hsee of the University of Chicago. (28 words)
cn: 芝加哥大学的奚恺元说，发现的驱动力深深植根于人类，与对食物或住所的基本驱动力非常相似。

en: Curiosity is often considered a good instinct—it can lead to new scientific advances, for instance—but sometimes such inquiry can backfire. (22 words)
cn: 好奇心常被视为一种好的本能——例如，它能带来新的科学进步——但有时，这种探究也会适得其反。

en: The insight that curiosity can drive you to do self-destructive things is a profound one. (15 words)
cn: 好奇心可能驱使你做出自我毁灭之事，这种洞见是深刻的。

en: Unhealthy curiosity is possible to resist, however. (7 words)
cn: 然而，不健康的好奇心是可能被抵制的。

en: In a final experiment, participants who were encouraged to predict how they would feel after viewing an unpleasant picture were less likely to choose to see such an image. (29 words)
cn: 在最后一项实验中，那些被鼓励去预测自己看完不愉快图片后会有什么感受的参与者，选择观看此类图片的可能性更低。

en: These results suggest that imagining the outcome of following through on one's curiosity ahead of time can help determine whether it is worth the endeavor. (25 words)
cn: 这些结果表明，提前想象一下循着好奇心走到底的结果，有助于判断是否值得付出这番努力。

en: "Thinking about long-term consequences is key to reducing the possible negative effects of curiosity," Hsee says. (16 words)
cn: “思考长远后果，是减少好奇心可能带来的负面影响的关键，”奚恺元说。

en: In other words, don't read online comments. (7 words)
cn: 换句话说，别看网上评论。

en: Weighing yourself regularly is a wonderful way to stay aware of any significant weight fluctuations. (15 words)
cn: 定期称体重是让自己随时了解任何显著体重波动的好方法。

en: However, when done too often, this habit can sometimes hurt more than it helps. (14 words)
cn: 然而，如果做得太频繁，这个习惯有时弊大于利。

en: As for me, weighing myself every day caused me to shift my focus from being generally healthy and physically active to focusing solely on the scale. (26 words)
cn: 就我而言，每天称体重导致我将注意力从保持总体健康和身体活跃，转移到只盯着体重秤上。

en: That was bad for my overall fitness goals. (8 words)
cn: 那对我整体的健身目标是不利的。

en: I had gained weight in the form of muscle mass, but thinking only of lowering the number on the scale, I altered my training program. (25 words)
cn: 我体重增加是因为肌肉量增长了，但因为一心只想着降低秤上的数字，我改变了自己的训练计划。

en: That conflicted with how I needed to train to reach my goals. (12 words)
cn: 这与我为达到目标而需要进行的训练方式相冲突。

en: I also found that weighing myself daily did not provide an accurate depiction of the hard work and progress I was making in the gym. (25 words)
cn: 我还发现，每天称体重并不能准确反映我在健身房里付出的努力和取得的进步。

en: It takes about three weeks to a month to notice any significant changes in your weight due to altering your training program. (22 words)
cn: 由于训练计划的改变，大约需要三周到一个月的时间才能注意到体重的任何显著变化。

en: The most immediate changes will be observed in skill level, strength, and inches lost. (14 words)
cn: 最直接的变化将体现在技巧水平、力量和围度的减少上。

en: For these reasons, I stopped weighing myself every day and switched to a bimonthly weighing schedule instead. (17 words)
cn: 出于这些原因，我不再每天称体重，而是改成每两周称一次。

en: Since weight loss is not my goal, it is less important for me to track my weight each week. (19 words)
cn: 既然减重不是我的目标，每周记录体重对我来说就不那么重要了。

en: Weighing every other week allows me to observe and account for any significant weight changes. (15 words)
cn: 隔周称重让我能观察到并解释任何显著的体重变化。

en: That tells me whether I need to adjust my training program. (11 words)
cn: 那会告诉我是否需要调整我的训练计划。

en: I use my bimonthly weigh-in results to get information about my nutrition as well. (14 words)
cn: 我也利用每两周一次的称重结果来获取有关我营养状况的信息。

en: If my training intensity remains the same, but I'm constantly hungry and dropping weight, this is a sign that I need to increase my daily caloric intake. (27 words)
cn: 如果我的训练强度保持不变，但我却总是感到饥饿且体重下降，这是一个信号，表明我需要增加每日热量摄入。

en: The decision to stop weighing myself every day has done wonders for my overall health, fitness, and well-being. (18 words)
cn: 不再每天称体重的决定，对我的总体健康、身体素质和幸福感产生了奇效。

en: I'm experiencing increased zeal for working out since I no longer carry the burden of a disappointing morning weigh-in. (19 words)
cn: 由于不再背负早晨称重结果令人失望的包袱，我对锻炼的热情更高了。

en: I've also experienced greater success in achieving my specific fitness goals because I'm training according to those goals, not the numbers on a scale. (24 words)
cn: 我在实现特定健身目标方面也取得了更大的成功，因为我是根据那些目标而非体重秤上的数字在进行训练。

en: Rather than obsessing over the scale, turn your focus to how you look, feel, how your clothes fit, and your overall energy level. (23 words)
cn: 与其纠结于体重秤，不如把注意力转向你的外表、感受、衣服是否合身以及你的整体精力水平。

en: Being a good parent is what every parent would like to be. (12 words)
cn: 做个好家长是每个父母的心愿。

en: But defining what it means to be a good parent is undoubtedly very tricky, particularly since children respond differently to the same style of parenting. (25 words)
cn: 但界定好家长意味着什么无疑十分棘手，尤其是因为孩子对同一种育儿方式的反应各不相同。

en: A calm, rule-following child might respond better to a different sort of parenting than, for example, a younger one. (19 words)
cn: 比如说，一个沉静守规矩的孩子，可能对不同于年幼孩子的那种育儿方式反应更好。

en: Fortunately, there's another sort of parent that's easier to describe: a patient parent. (13 words)
cn: 幸运的是，还有另一种更容易描述的家长：耐心的家长。

en: Children of every age benefit from patient parenting. (8 words)
cn: 每个年龄段的孩子都受益于耐心的养育。

en: Still, while every parent would like to be patient, this is no easy task. (14 words)
cn: 尽管如此，虽然每位家长都想变得有耐心，但这绝非易事。

en: Sometimes, parents get exhausted and are unable to maintain a tolerant style with their kids. (15 words)
cn: 有时，父母疲惫不堪，便无法对孩子保持宽容的态度。

en: I understand this. (3 words)
cn: 我理解这一点。

en: You're only human, and sometimes your kids can push you just a little too far. (15 words)
cn: 你只是个凡人，有时你的孩子就是能把你逼得稍稍过了头。

en: And then the inevitable happens: You lose your patience and either scream at your kids or say something that was too harsh and does nobody any good. (27 words)
cn: 然后不可避免的事发生了：你失去了耐心，要么对孩子大吼大叫，要么说了什么太伤人、对谁都没好处的话。

en: You wish that you could turn back the clock and start over. (12 words)
cn: 你多希望时光可以倒流，从头来过。

en: We've all been there. (4 words)
cn: 我们都经历过这种情形。

en: However, even though it's common, it's vital to keep in mind that in a single moment of fatigue, you can say something to your child that you may regret for a long time. (33 words)
cn: 然而，尽管这很常见，但必须牢记，就在感到疲惫的那一瞬间，你可能对孩子说出让自己后悔许久的话。

en: This may not only do damage to your relationship with your child but also affect your child's self-esteem. (18 words)
cn: 这不仅可能损害你与孩子的关系，还会影响孩子的自尊心。

en: If you consistently lose your cool with your kids, then you are modeling a lack of emotional control for your kids. (21 words)
cn: 如果你经常对孩子失控，那你就是在给孩子示范什么叫情绪缺乏自控。

en: We are all becoming increasingly aware of the importance of modeling patience for the younger generation. (16 words)
cn: 我们大家都越来越意识到为年轻一代树立耐心榜样的重要性。

en: This is a skill that will help them all throughout life. (11 words)
cn: 这是一项将使他们终生受益的技能。

en: In fact, the ability to maintain emotional control when confronted by stress is one of the most significant of all life's skills. (22 words)
cn: 实际上，面对压力时保持情绪控制的能力，是所有生活技能中最重要的一项。

en: Certainly, it's hard to maintain patience at all times with your kids. (12 words)
cn: 当然，任何时候都对孩子保持耐心是很难的。

en: A more practical goal is to try to be as calm as you can when faced with trying situations involving your children. (22 words)
cn: 一个更实际的目标是，在遇到涉及孩子的棘手情况时，尽量保持冷静。

en: I can promise you this: As a result of working toward this goal, you and your children will benefit and emerge from stressful moments feeling better physically and emotionally. (29 words)
cn: 我可以向你保证：朝着这个目标努力的结果是，你和你的孩子都会受益，并在走出紧张时刻时身心都感到更好。

en: It is not difficult to set targets for staff. (9 words)
cn: 给员工设定目标并不难。

en: It is much harder, however, to understand their negative consequences. (10 words)
cn: 然而，要理解它们带来的负面后果就难多了。

en: Most work-related behaviors have multiple components. (6 words)
cn: 大多数与工作相关的行为都有多个构成要素。

en: Emphasize one and the others become distorted. (7 words)
cn: 强调其一，其余的就会扭曲变形。

en: Travel on a London bus and you'll quickly see how this works with drivers. (14 words)
cn: 坐一趟伦敦的公交车，你很快就能看出这在司机身上是如何应验的。

en: Watch people get on and show their tickets. (8 words)
cn: 观察人们上车并出示车票。

en: Are they carefully inspected? (4 words)
cn: 票会被仔细检查吗？

en: Never. (1 words)
cn: 从来没有。

en: Do people get on without paying? (6 words)
cn: 有人不付钱就上车吗？

en: Of course! (2 words)
cn: 当然有！

en: Are there inspectors to check that people have paid? (9 words)
cn: 有没有检查员来查票呢？

en: Possibly, but very few. (4 words)
cn: 可能有，但寥寥无几。

en: And people who run for the bus? (7 words)
cn: 那些追赶公交车的人呢？

en: They are ignored. (3 words)
cn: 他们被无视了。

en: How about jumping lights? (4 words)
cn: 闯红灯呢？

en: Buses do so almost as frequently as cyclists. (8 words)
cn: 公交车闯红灯几乎和骑自行车的人一样频繁。

en: Why? (1 words)
cn: 为什么？

en: Because the target is punctuality. (5 words)
cn: 因为目标是准点率。

en: People complained that buses were late and infrequent. (8 words)
cn: 人们抱怨公交车晚点且班次太少。

en: So, the number of buses and bus lanes were increased, and drivers were rewarded or punished according to the time they took. (22 words)
cn: 于是，公交车和公交专用道的数量增加了，司机也根据他们所用的时间而受到奖惩。

en: And drivers hit these targets. (5 words)
cn: 司机们达到了这些目标。

en: But they also hit cyclists. (5 words)
cn: 但他们在路上也撞了骑车人。

en: If the target was changed to revenue, you would have more inspectors and more sensitive pricing. (16 words)
cn: 如果把目标改为收入，就会有更多的检查员和更灵敏的定价。

en: If the criterion changed to safety, you would get more cautious drivers who obeyed traffic laws. (16 words)
cn: 如果标准改为安全性，你就会得到更谨慎且遵守交通法规的司机。

en: But both these criteria would be at the expense of time. (11 words)
cn: 但这两种标准都会以牺牲时间为代价。

en: There is another problem: people become immensely inventive in hitting targets. (11 words)
cn: 还有一个问题：人们在达成目标方面会变得极有创造力。

en: Have you noticed that you can leave on a flight an hour late but still arrive on time? (18 words)
cn: 你是否注意到，你乘坐的航班可能晚起飞一小时，却仍然准时到达？

en: Airlines have simply changed the time a trip is meant to take. (12 words)
cn: 航空公司只是更改了航程原本应需的时间。

en: A one-hour flight is now billed as a two-hour flight. (10 words)
cn: 一小时的航程现在被标成两小时。

en: The moral of the story is simple. (7 words)
cn: 故事的寓意很简单。

en: Most jobs are multidimensional, with multiple criteria. (7 words)
cn: 大多数工作都是多维度的，有着多重标准。

en: Choose one criterion and you may well sacrifice others. (9 words)
cn: 选择一个标准，你很可能会牺牲其他标准。

en: Everything can be done faster and made cheaper, but there is a cost. (13 words)
cn: 一切都可以做得更快、变得更便宜，但这是有代价的。

en: Setting targets can and does have unforeseen negative consequences. (9 words)
cn: 设定目标可能而且确实会产生无法预见的负面后果。

en: This is not an argument against target-setting. (7 words)
cn: 这并不是反对目标设定的论据。

en: But it is an argument for exploring consequences first. (9 words)
cn: 但这是主张先探究后果的论据。

en: All good targets should have multiple criteria relating to critical factors such as time, money, quality, and customer feedback. (19 words)
cn: 所有好的目标都应该包含与时间、金钱、质量和客户反馈等关键因素相关的多重标准。

en: The trick is not only to specify just one or even two dimensions of the objective, but also to understand how to help people better achieve the objective. (28 words)
cn: 诀窍在于不仅要明确目标的一个甚至两个维度，还在于理解如何帮助人们更好地实现目标。

en: Harlan Coben believes that if you're a writer, you'll find the time; and that if you can't find the time, then writing isn't a priority and you're not a writer. (30 words)
cn: 哈兰·科本认为，如果你是作家，你总会找到时间；如果你找不到时间，那么写作就不是你优先考虑的事，你也算不上作家。

en: For him, writing is a normal job—a job like any other. (12 words)
cn: 对他来说，写作是一份普通的工作——和其他任何工作一样。

en: He has compared it with plumbing, pointing out that a plumber doesn't wake up and say that he can't work with pipes today. (23 words)
cn: 他将其与管道工做了比较，指出管道工不会早上醒来就说自己今天没法跟管道打交道了。

en: If, like most writers these days, you're holding down a job to pay the bills, it's not easy to find the time to write. (24 words)
cn: 如果你像当今大多数作家一样，正为支付账单而保住一份工作，那就很难找到写作的时间。

en: But it's not impossible. (4 words)
cn: 但这并非不可能。

en: It requires determination and single-mindedness. (5 words)
cn: 它需要决心和专注。

en: Remember that most bestselling authors began writing when they were doing other things to earn a living. (17 words)
cn: 请记住，大多数畅销书作家开始写作时都在做其他事情来谋生。

en: And today, even writers who are fairly well-known often have to do other work to supplement their writing income. (19 words)
cn: 而如今，即使是相当知名的作家，也常常不得不做其他工作来补贴写作收入。

en: As Harlan Coben has suggested, it's a question of priorities. (10 words)
cn: 正如哈兰·科本所说，这是个优先次序的问题。

en: To make writing a priority, you'll have to sacrifice some of your day-to-day activities and some things you really enjoy. (20 words)
cn: 要让写作成为优先事项，你就必须牺牲一些日常活动和一些你真正喜欢的事。

en: Depending on your interests and your lifestyle, that might mean spending less time watching television or listening to music, though some people can write while they listen to music. (29 words)
cn: 根据你的兴趣和生活方式，那可能意味着减少看电视或听音乐的时间，尽管有些人能边听音乐边写作。

en: You might have to cut down on the amount of exercise or sport you do. (15 words)
cn: 你可能必须减少锻炼或运动的量。

en: You'll have to make social media an occasional activity rather than a daily, time-consuming habit. (15 words)
cn: 你必须让社交媒体成为一种偶尔的活动，而不是每天耗费时间的习惯。

en: There'll probably be less socializing with your friends and less time with your family. (14 words)
cn: 与朋友的社交和与家人共度的时光大概都会减少。

en: It's a tough learning curve, and it won't always make you popular. (12 words)
cn: 这是一个艰难的学习过程，而且它不见得总让你受人欢迎。

en: There's just one thing you should try to keep at least sometime for—in addition to your writing—and that's reading. (21 words)
cn: 除了写作之外，只有一件事你至少该设法留点时间给它——那就是阅读。

en: Any writer needs to read as much and as widely as they can, it's the one indispensable supporter—something you can't do without. (23 words)
cn: 任何作家都需要尽可能多、尽可能广地阅读，这是一个不可或缺的支撑——你无法缺少的东西。

en: Time is finite, the older you get, the quicker it seems to go. (13 words)
cn: 时间有限，年岁越长，它似乎流逝得越快。

en: We need to use it so carefully and as productively as we can. (13 words)
cn: 我们需要尽可能谨慎并富有成效地利用它。

en: That means prioritizing our activities so that we spend most time on the things we really want to do. (19 words)
cn: 那意味着要确定活动的优先级，以便把大部分时间花在我们真正想做的事情上。

en: If you are a writer, that means—above all writing. (10 words)
cn: 如果你是作家，那意味着——首要之事便是写作。

en: Here's a common scenario that any number of entrepreneurs face today: you're the CEO of a small business, and though you're making a nice profit, you need to find a way to take it to the next level. (38 words)
cn: 这里有当今无数企业家都会面临的一个常见情景：你是一家小企业的CEO，虽然利润不错，但你需要找到方法将其提升到新的水平。

en: What you need to do is prioritize growth by establishing a growth team. (13 words)
cn: 你需要做的是，通过建立一个增长团队来将增长作为优先要务。

en: A growth team is made up of members from different departments within your company, and it harnesses the power of collaboration to focus exclusively on finding ways to grow. (29 words)
cn: 增长团队由公司内不同部门的成员组成，它利用协作的力量，专门致力于寻找增长途径。

en: Let's look at a real-world example. (6 words)
cn: 让我们看一个真实的例子。

en: Prior to forming a growth team, the software company BitTorrent had 50 employees working in the traditional departments of engineering, marketing, and product development. (24 words)
cn: 在组建增长团队之前，软件公司BitTorrent有50名员工在工程、营销和产品开发等传统部门工作。

en: This brought them good results until 2012, when their growth plateaued. (11 words)
cn: 这给他们带来了良好的业绩，直到2012年增长趋于停滞。

en: The problem was that too many customers were using the basic, free version of their product. (16 words)
cn: 问题在于，太多的客户在使用他们产品的基本免费版。

en: And despite improvements to the premium, paid version, few people were making the upgrade. (14 words)
cn: 而且，尽管高端付费版本有所改进，却几乎没什么人升级。

en: Things changed, however, when an innovative project-marketing manager came aboard, created a growth team, and sparked the kind of fresh perspective they needed. (23 words)
cn: 然而，当一位富有创新精神的项目营销经理加入，创建了一个增长团队，并激发了他们所需的那种全新视角时，情况发生了改变。

en: By looking at engineering issues from a marketing point of view, it became clear that the lack of upgrades wasn't due to a quality issue. (25 words)
cn: 通过从营销的角度审视工程问题，事情变得很清楚：升级的缺乏并非源于质量问题。

en: Most customers were simply unaware of the premium version and what it offered. (13 words)
cn: 大多数客户压根就不知道高级版的存在及其提供的功能。

en: Armed with this insight, the marketing and engineering teams joined forces to raise awareness by prominently promoting the premium version to users of the free version. (26 words)
cn: 有了这一深刻见解，营销和工程团队通力合作，通过向免费版用户重点推广高级版来提升认知度。

en: As a result, upgrades skyrocketed, and revenue increased by 92 percent. (11 words)
cn: 结果，升级量猛增，收入增长了92%。

en: But in order for your growth team to succeed, it needs to have a strong leader. (16 words)
cn: 但为了让你的增长团队成功，需要有一位强有力的领导者。

en: It needs someone who can unite the interdisciplinary team and keep them on course for improvement. (16 words)
cn: 它需要一个能将这个跨学科团队团结起来，并让他们在改进道路上方向不偏的人。

en: This leader will identify the target area, set clear goals, and establish a time frame for the accomplishment of these goals. (21 words)
cn: 这位领导者将确定目标领域，设定明确的目标，并为实现这些目标规定时间期限。

en: The growth leader is also responsible for keeping the team focused on moving forward and steering them clear of distractions. (20 words)
cn: 增长团队的领导者还负责让团队专注于向前推进，并引导他们避开让人分心之事。

en: While attractive new ideas can be distracting, the team leader must recognize when these ideas don't serve the current goal and need to be put on the back burner. (29 words)
cn: 虽然诱人的新点子会令人分心，但团队领导必须识别出这些点子何时对当前目标无益，并需要将其搁置。

en: Your social life is defined as the activities you do with other people, for pleasure, when you are not working. (20 words)
cn: 你的社交生活，被定义为你在工作之余、为消遣而与他人共同进行的活动。

en: It is important to have a social life, but what is right for one person won't be right for another. (20 words)
cn: 拥有社交生活很重要，但适合一个人的方式未必适合另一个人。

en: Some of us feel energized by spending lots of time with others, whereas some of us may feel drained, even if it's doing something we enjoy. (26 words)
cn: 我们中有些人因花大量时间与人共处而充满活力，而另一些人则可能感到精疲力竭，即使在做着喜欢的事。

en: This is why finding a balance in your social life is key. (12 words)
cn: 这就是为什么在社交生活中找到平衡是关键。

en: Spending too much time on your own, not seeing others, can make you feel lonely and disconnected. (17 words)
cn: 花太多时间独处、不与别人见面，会让你感到孤独和疏离。

en: Loneliness is known to impact on your mental health and contribute to a low mood. (15 words)
cn: 众所周知，孤独会影响你的心理健康，并导致情绪低落。

en: Anyone can feel lonely at any time. (7 words)
cn: 任何人在任何时候都可能感到孤独。

en: This might be especially true if, for example you are working from home and you are missing out on the social conversations that happen in the office. (27 words)
cn: 举例来说，如果你正在家办公，错过了发生在办公室里的社交对话，这可能尤其真切。

en: Other life changes also trigger periods of loneliness too, such as retirement, changing a job or becoming a parent. (19 words)
cn: 其他的人生转变也会触发孤独期，比如退休、换工作或初为人父母。

en: It's important to recognize feelings or loneliness. (7 words)
cn: 识别出孤独的感受很重要。

en: There are ways to regain a social life. (8 words)
cn: 是有办法重获社交生活的。

en: But it be overwhelming at first. (6 words)
cn: 但一开始可能会令人望而生畏。

en: You can then find groups and activities related to those where you will be able to meet like-minded people. (19 words)
cn: 然后你可以找到与那些兴趣相关的团体和活动，在那里你将能遇到志同道合的人。

en: There are groups aimed at new parents, at those who want to try a new sport for the first time or networking events for those in the same profession to meet up and share ideas. (35 words)
cn: 有针对新手父母的团体，有面向首次尝试新运动者的团体，还有为同行职业人士举办、以便见面交流想法的社交活动。

en: On the other hand, it is also possible to have too much of a social life. (16 words)
cn: 另一方面，社交生活过多也是有可能的。

en: If you feel like you're always doing something and there is never any space in your calendar for downtime, you could suffer social burnout or social fatigue. (28 words)
cn: 如果你感觉自己总是在忙，日程表上从未给休息留出空间，那你可能正遭受社交倦怠或社交疲劳。

en: We all have our own social limit and it's important to recognize when you're feeling like it's all too much. (20 words)
cn: 我们都有自己的社交极限，重要的是，当你觉得社交多得有些过分时，能认识到这一点。

en: Low mood, low energy, irritability and trouble sleeping could be signs of poor social health. (15 words)
cn: 情绪低落、精力不济、易怒和睡眠困难可能是社交健康状况不佳的迹象。

en: Make sure you mark out sometime in your diary when you're unavailable for socialising and use this time to relax, rest and recover. (23 words)
cn: 务必在你日记中划出一些不便社交的时间，并用这段时间来放松、休息和恢复。

en: There are many understandable reasons why you might find it difficult to ask for help when you need it. (19 words)
cn: 有许多可以理解的原因，导致你可能在需要时觉得难以开口求助。

en: Psychologists have been interested in this question for decades, not least because people's widespread reluctance to ask for help has led to some high-profile failures. (27 words)
cn: 几十年来，心理学家一直对这个问题感兴趣，一个相当重要的原因是，人们普遍不愿求助已导致了一些引人注目的失败。

en: Asking for help takes courage. (5 words)
cn: 开口求助需要勇气。

en: It involves communicating a need on your part—there's something you can't do. (13 words)
cn: 这涉及到传达出你的一种需要——有些事你做不来。

en: In other words, you're broadcasting your own weaknesses, which can be uncomfortable. (12 words)
cn: 换句话说，你在广播自己的弱点，而这会令人不自在。

en: You might worry about coming across as incompetent. (8 words)
cn: 你可能会担心给人留下无能的印象。

en: You might have concerns about losing control of whatever it is you're asking for help with. (16 words)
cn: 你可能会担心，无论求助什么事，自己都会对它失去控制。

en: Once someone starts to help, perhaps they'll take over, or get credit for your earlier efforts. (16 words)
cn: 一旦有人开始帮忙，或许他们会接手，或者抢走你先前努力的功劳。

en: Yet another factor that you might be worried about is being a nuisance or inconveniencing the person you go to for help. (23 words)
cn: 然而，你可能担忧的另一个因素是，你会成为求助对象的麻烦，或给他们带去不便。

en: If you struggle with low self-esteem, you might find it especially difficult to reach out for help because you have the added worry of the other person declining your request. (32 words)
cn: 如果你苦于自卑，可能会觉得向外求助格外困难，因为你额外又多了一重担忧：对方会拒绝你的请求。

en: You might see such refusals as implying something negative about the status of your relationship with them. (17 words)
cn: 你可能会将这类拒绝视作暗示着你和他们之间的关系状态有某种负面之处。

en: To overcome these difficulties, try to remind yourself that everyone needs help sometimes. (13 words)
cn: 要克服这些困难，试着提醒自己，每个人都有需要帮助的时候。

en: Nobody knows everything and can do everything all by themselves. (10 words)
cn: 没有人能知晓一切，并全靠自己做成所有事。

en: And while you might fear coming across as incompetent, there's actually research that shows that advice-seekers are perceived as more competent, not less. (24 words)
cn: 而且，虽然你可能害怕留下无能的印象，但实际上有研究表明，寻求建议者会被视为更有能力，而非相反。

en: Perhaps most encouraging of all is a paper from 2022 by researchers at Stanford University that involved a mix of contrived help-seeking interactions and asking people to recall times they'd sought help in the past. (37 words)
cn: 或许最令人鼓舞的，是斯坦福大学研究人员在2022年发表的一篇论文，该论文既涉及人为设计的求助互动，又让人们回忆过往求助的经历。

en: The findings showed that help-seekers generally underestimate how willing other people will be to help and how good it'll make the help-giver feel (for most people, having the chance to help someone is highly rewarding). (37 words)
cn: 研究结果表明，求助者通常低估了别人提供帮助的意愿，以及这会给帮助者带来多好的感受（对大多数人来说，有机会帮助别人是件极有回报感的事）。

en: So, bear all this in mind the next time you need to ask for help. (16 words)
cn: 所以，下次你需要开口求助时，请将这一切铭记在心。

en: Also, take care over who you ask and when you ask them. (12 words)
cn: 此外，向谁求助、何时求助，也要留心斟酌。

en: And if someone can't help right now, avoid taking it personally. (11 words)
cn: 而且，如果有人此刻无法帮忙，不要往心里去。

en: They might just be too busy, or they might not feel confident about their ability to help. (17 words)
cn: 他们可能只是太忙了，或者对自己帮忙的能力没有信心。

en: Most of us strive for self-respect—to see ourselves in a positive light and to be proud of who we are. (20 words)
cn: 我们大多数人都努力追求自尊——以积极的眼光看待自己，并为自己是谁感到自豪。

en: Any comments or experiences that undermine those feelings of self-worth can be uncomfortable and it's only natural to want to avoid them. (23 words)
cn: 任何有损这种自我价值感的评论或经历，都可能令人不适，而想要避开它们也完全合乎人之常情。

en: But the thing is, whether your self-worth really is being undermined is, to a large degree, subjective. (17 words)
cn: 但问题是，你的自我价值是否真的受到了损害，在很大程度上是主观的。

en: Much of it comes down to your perception of what someone said or did. (14 words)
cn: 这在很大程度上归结为你对某人言行举止的感知。

en: When you take things personally, you're interpreting these situations as saying something significant and negative about you as a person. (20 words)
cn: 当你对事情耿耿于怀时，你是在将这些情况解读为，它们表明了你这个人本身有某种重大且负面的东西。

en: Say your boss picks holes in your sales report, and you come away feeling like this means she thinks you're a poor member of staff. (27 words)
cn: 比方说，你的老板在你的销售报告中挑刺，而你离开时觉得这意味着她认为你是个差劲的员工。

en: Or, two of your friends cancel your planned get-together at the pub and you interpret this as meaning they're bored of you. (23 words)
cn: 或者，你的两个朋友取消了原定在酒吧的聚会，你把这解读为她们对你感到厌烦了。

en: Psychologists have a term for this way of thinking: personalisation. (10 words)
cn: 心理学家对这种思维方式有个专门的术语：个人化。

en: But the reality is that your boss might view you very highly. (12 words)
cn: 但现实可能是，你的老板可能对你评价很高。

en: It's just you made some mistakes in the report and she wanted to point these out so that you do better next time. (24 words)
cn: 只不过你在报告中出了一些错，她想指出来，好让你下次做得更好。

en: Similarly, in the case of your friends cancelling, perhaps they both felt unwell that weekend or they just changed their minds. (21 words)
cn: 同样，在你朋友取消聚会的情况下，也许她们那个周末都感觉不舒服，或者只是改变主意了而已。

en: In both examples — and others that we all experience — there's another process at work that psychologists call mind reading. (20 words)
cn: 在这两个例子——以及我们都经历过的其他例子——中，还有另一个心理过程在起作用，心理学家称之为读心术。

en: This is when you assume to know what other people are thinking about you, when you can't really know without asking them directly. (24 words)
cn: 这就是当你假定自己知道别人在想你什么，而你不直接询问他们的话，其实是无法真正知道的。

en: One simple way to avoid engaging in personalisation and mind reading is to challenge your negative assumptions of what's happened. (20 words)
cn: 避免陷入个人化和读心术的一个简单方法，就是挑战你对所发生之事的负面假设。

en: When you find yourself taking a situation personally, try to think of other interpretations that are less focused on you—especially in the sense of saying anything deep or fundamental about you. (33 words)
cn: 当你发觉自己对某个情况耿耿于怀时，试着想想其他没那么聚焦于你自身的解释——尤其不要把事情理解为在深层或根本上是在说你。

en: When you do this, it might help to try to put yourself in other people's shoes. (17 words)
cn: 当你这样做时，试着设身处地替别人着想也许会有所帮助。

en: The fact your boss took the time to give you feedback at all means they care enough to want to help you improve. (24 words)
cn: 你的老板花费时间给你反馈，这一事实本身就意味着他们足够关心你，想要帮助你改进。

en: When it comes to your friends — maybe it's because your friendship is so strong that they felt able to cancel at late notice. (25 words)
cn: 至于你的朋友们——也许正是因为你们的友谊如此牢固，她们才觉得临时通知取消也无妨。
